message.content: <local-command-caveat>Caveat: The messages below were generated by the user while running local commands. DO NOT respond to these messages or otherwise consider them in your response unless the user explicitly asks you to.</local-command-caveat>

Mount heat A-. Read the paddock for current state and triage decisions
  made in prior chat.

  Then read BUS0 §Remote Node Access — the new spec backbone, near the
  end of Tools/buk/vov_veiled/BUS0-BashUtilitiesSpec.adoc — plus the three
  platform subdocs:
    Tools/buk/vov_veiled/BUSNW-NodeWindows.adoc  (Windows, elaborate)
    Tools/buk/vov_veiled/BUSNL-NodeLinux.adoc    (stub)
    Tools/buk/vov_veiled/BUSNM-NodeMac.adoc      (stub)

  Context: this heat accumulated paces and tooling under several earlier
  design assumptions before the BUS0 backbone landed. Many surfaces
  (tabtargets, handbook procedures, modules) were drafted against
  older assumptions and need to be audited against the new spec. The
  paddock summarizes triage decisions from chat; your job is to apply
  that triage to actual code/docs/tabtargets and produce a concrete
  restructuring plan.

  Tasks:

  1. Survey existing surfaces that touch this heat's domain:
       - buw-HW* family (Windows handbook + automation tabtargets)
       - buw-rh* family (BURN regime ops; rename target buw-rn*)
       - buw-rhc* constructors (per-platform profile creators)
       - buw-wcb, buw-wck, buw-rhk (recent split tabtargets)
       - rbw-HW* RBK-side handbooks
       - buhw_windows.sh, buwc_windows.sh, buwc_cli.sh modules
       - Any handbook prose under buw-HWab/HWar/HWax (the access* trio)

  2. Audit each surface against BUS0 §Remote Node Access. For each:
       - Implements a tabtarget the spec calls for → keep (possibly rename
         per new colophon scheme buw-n[pw]*)
       - Implements an obsolete approach the new design replaces → mark
         for retirement
       - Spans both privileged and workload concerns conflicting with the
         tier model → mark for split

  3. Specifically identify handbooks/human procedures that:
       - Survive: the manual console-phase step (operator enabling temp
         password auth at Windows keyboard) is the only handbook-residue
         still needed
       - Should morph: thin wrappers pointing at new automation
       - Should delete: replaced wholesale by Garrison/Conscript ceremony

  4. Propose the restructured pace plan for ₣A-:
       - Confirm which pending paces survive triage as already discussed
         in paddock
       - Slate new paces needed for implementation: BURH→BURN code-side
         rename, new colophon families, Garrison/Conscript machinery,
         handbook retirement/morphing
       - Sequence: spec subdocs (AAR/AAQ already pending) → implementation
         → practice walk (AAC reslated) → downstream practice (AAD/E/F)
         → regression (AAP)

  Output: a triage report listing each existing surface with verdict
  (keep / rename / morph / retire / split), plus the proposed list of
  new paces with brief dockets. Don't execute changes — propose for
  review.

## vvx
Job Jockey Kit - MCP tools for project initiative management

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Docket:
  ## Character
  Human-driven with agent amending — running automation tabtargets on real hardware, discovering gaps.
  
  ## Docket
  Run the new SSH automation tabtargets against the actual Windows host.
  
  **Mac side:**
  1. Run platform constructors per BURH profile — creates `burh.env`, displays `ssh-keygen` commands if pubkey missing
  2. User runs `ssh-keygen` manually (copy/paste from constructor output)
  3. Re-run constructors — picks up `.pub` files, writes complete `burh.env`
  4. `buw-HWsc` — write `~/.ssh/config` entries from all profiles
  5. Commit + push (BURH profiles now carry real pubkeys)
  
  **Windows/WSL side:**
  Git pull in WSL, then run `buw-HWbs`.
  
  Note: sshd is already configured from manual AccessBase practice (steps 1-7). Running `buw-HWbs` will overwrite `sshd_config` and `authorized_keys` with BURH-derived versions. This validates that automation produces a working result, but replaces manual config. Human decides.
  
  **Verify:**
  - `buw-HWvs` per profile — ssh via each alias, confirm correct environment lands
  - `jjx_bind` to Windows host — confirm relay works
  
  Agent amends tabtarget code for any issues discovered during practice. This is the first real cross-boundary execution — expect discoveries.

content.content:
1	# jjezs_paddock ₣A-
2	
3	## Goal
4	
5	Windows test infrastructure for Recipe Bottle. Run RB's container isolation
6	tests on a Windows host with WSL, Cygwin, and dual Docker daemons.
7	
8	## Current Design
9	
10	The architectural backbone lives in BUS0 §Remote Node Access (`Tools/buk/vov_veiled/BUS0-BashUtilitiesSpec.adoc`)
11	plus three platform subdocs (`BUSNW-NodeWindows.adoc`, `BUSNL-NodeLinux.adoc`,
12	`BUSNM-NodeMac.adoc`). Read BUS0 §Remote Node Access first; this paddock
13	captures only state that doesn't fit there.
14	
15	**Header decisions made in this heat:**
16	
17	- BURH regime renamed to BURN (n = Node)
18	- BURN_TIER field added: `privileged | workload`
19	- Two-tier model:
20	  - Privileged tier: node-level ops (sshd config, user provisioning).
21	    Multi-resident allowed; one operator at a time by social contract.
22	  - Workload tier: user-level ops (normal remote work). Exclusive to one
23	    station user; ephemeral.
24	- Verb vocabulary (BUSN quoins): Garrison / Conscript / Discharge / Inventory
25	- Tabtarget colophon families under `buw-`:
26	  - `buw-rn{l,r,v}` — BURN regime ops (list / render / validate)
27	  - `buw-npg{l,m,c,w,p,x}` — Garrison per platform (privileged setup)
28	  - `buw-nwc{l,m,c,w,p,x}` — Conscript per platform (workload mint)
29	  - `buw-nw{d,i}` — Discharge / Inventory
30	  - `buw-nw{c,r,s}` — Check / Run / Ssh (workload operational)
31	  - `buw-hn0` — Handbook landing for node ops
32	- Visual proximity note: `buw-nwc` (Check) vs `buw-nwc{platform}` (Conscript)
33	  — distinct colophons, accepted
34	
35	## Heat Sequence (post-triage)
36	
37	Spec exists. Remaining: implement, walk, practice.
38	
39	Pending:
40	
41	- **Implement** the BUS0 tabtarget set (BURH → BURN code rename, new
42	  colophon families, Garrison/Conscript/Discharge/Inventory machinery)
43	- **₢A-AAC** reslate to: walk access ceremony on real hardware
44	- **₢A-AAD** practice WSL + Cygwin install on Windows host
45	- **₢A-AAE** practice fundus provisioning inside WSL
46	- **₢A-AAF** practice Docker dual-daemon setup
47	- **₢A-AAP** regression-retest fundus after practice amendments
48	- **₢A-AAR** specify BUWC module + key builder in BUS0
49	- **₢A-AAQ** specify per-command subdocs (burn_install_key etc.)
50	
51	Triaged out:
52

# jjezs_paddock ₣A-

## Goal

Windows test infrastructure for Recipe Bottle. Run RB's container isolation
tests on a Windows host with WSL, Cygwin, and dual Docker daemons.

## Current Design

The architectural backbone lives in BUS0 §Remote Node Access (`Tools/buk/vov_veiled/BUS0-BashUtilitiesSpec.adoc`)
plus three platform subdocs (`BUSNW-NodeWindows.adoc`, `BUSNL-NodeLinux.adoc`,
`BUSNM-NodeMac.adoc`). Read BUS0 §Remote Node Access first; this paddock
captures only state that doesn't fit there.

**Header decisions made in this heat:**

- BURH regime renamed to BURN (n = Node)
- BURN_TIER field added: `privileged | workload`
- Two-tier model:
  - Privileged tier: node-level ops (sshd config, user provisioning).
    Multi-resident allowed; one operator at a time by social contract.
  - Workload tier: user-level ops (normal remote work). Exclusive to one
    station user; ephemeral.
- Verb vocabulary (BUSN quoins): Garrison / Conscript / Discharge / Inventory
- Tabtarget colophon families under `buw-`:
  - `buw-rn{l,r,v}` — BURN regime ops (list / render / validate)
  - `buw-npg{l,m,c,w,p,x}` — Garrison per platform (privileged setup)
  - `buw-nwc{l,m,c,w,p,x}` — Conscript per platform (workload mint)
  - `buw-nw{d,i}` — Discharge / Inventory
  - `buw-nw{c,r,s}` — Check / Run / Ssh (workload operational)
  - `buw-hn0` — Handbook landing for node ops
- Visual proximity note: `buw-nwc` (Check) vs `buw-nwc{platform}` (Conscript)
  — distinct colophons, accepted

## Heat Sequence (post-triage)

Spec exists. Remaining: implement, walk, practice.

Pending:

- **Implement** the BUS0 tabtarget set (BURH → BURN code rename, new
  colophon families, Garrison/Conscript/Discharge/Inventory machinery)
- **₢A-AAC** reslate to: walk access ceremony on real hardware
- **₢A-AAD** practice WSL + Cygwin install on Windows host
- **₢A-AAE** practice fundus provisioning inside WSL
- **₢A-AAF** practice Docker dual-daemon setup
- **₢A-AAP** regression-retest fundus after practice amendments
- **₢A-AAR** specify BUWC module + key builder in BUS0
- **₢A-AAQ** specify per-command subdocs (burn_install_key etc.)

Triaged out:

- ₢A-AAH (verification tabtargets) — fold into practice wrap notes
- ₢A-AAI (reconsider Cygwin install) — fold into ₢A-AAD wrap notes
- ₢A-AAJ (autonumber sweep) — relocate to ₣A6 (handbook restart)
- ₢A-AAS, ₢A-AAT (handbook-render fixture coverage) — relocate to ₣BB

## Deferred (named for future return)

- **Adopt** — cross-station-user pubkey install via existing privileged
  trust. Today: each station user runs the ceremony fresh from their
  own machine.
- **User regime** — centralized per-user pubkey for shared admin
  scenarios. Pairs naturally with Adopt when both are needed.
- **Git normalization on remote** — JJK has `jjx_plant`; BUK doesn't
  duplicate.

## Standing Notes

- Tailscale provides stable transport (Mac sees `rocket` as
  `100.x.y.z` / tailnet hostname); not adopted as a BUK dependency
- Windows username `b hyslop` (with space) — audit shell quoting at
  every `${BURN_USER}` expansion during practice
- Fundus capability registry: `Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSFR-FundusRegistry.md`
- PowerShell-from-WSL pattern: bash owns control flow, PowerShell is
  the Windows syscall layer; one atomic command per call
- BURS_USER station regime field routes profiles to
  `.buk/users/${BURS_USER}/` subdirectory
- Three Windows BURN profiles for `rocket` distinguished by
  `BURN_COMMAND`: cygwin (`...bash.exe -l`), WSL (`wsl.exe -d ...`),
  PowerShell (`powershell.exe`)

# jjezs_pace ₢A-AAC

## Character
Human-driven with agent amending — running automation tabtargets on real hardware, discovering gaps.

## Docket
Run the new SSH automation tabtargets against the actual Windows host.

**Mac side:**
1. Run platform constructors per BURH profile — creates `burh.env`, displays `ssh-keygen` commands if pubkey missing
2. User runs `ssh-keygen` manually (copy/paste from constructor output)
3. Re-run constructors — picks up `.pub` files, writes complete `burh.env`
4. `buw-HWsc` — write `~/.ssh/config` entries from all profiles
5. Commit + push (BURH profiles now carry real pubkeys)

**Windows/WSL side:**
Git pull in WSL, then run `buw-HWbs`.

Note: sshd is already configured from manual AccessBase practice (steps 1-7). Running `buw-HWbs` will overwrite `sshd_config` and `authorized_keys` with BURH-derived versions. This validates that automation produces a working result, but replaces manual config. Human decides.

**Verify:**
- `buw-HWvs` per profile — ssh via each alias, confirm correct environment lands
- `jjx_bind` to Windows host — confirm relay works

Agent amends tabtarget code for any issues discovered during practice. This is the first real cross-boundary execution — expect discoveries.

1283	== Remote Node Access
1284	
1285	Bringing remote nodes under SSH key control from a local station, establishing
1286	trust that supports both privileged node-level operations and ephemeral
1287	workload-tier work over the same {burn_regime}.
1288	
1289	=== Privilege Tiers
1290	
1291	The {burn_tier} field of every {burn_regime} profile classifies the profile
1292	as either `privileged` or `workload`. Tabtargets refuse aliases of the wrong
1293	tier at entry.
1294	
1295	==== Privileged Tier
1296	
1297	Privileged profiles authenticate as a remote OS user with admin or equivalent
1298	privileges. Used for node-level operations: sshd configuration, user
1299	provisioning, node-establishment ceremonies.
1300	
1301	Multiple station users may hold privileged trust on the same node (file
1302	format supports multi-resident keys). Operational convention: only one
1303	station user actively orchestrates work at a time. This convention is
1304	enforced by social contract, not BUK locking.
1305	
1306	==== Workload Tier
1307	
1308	Workload profiles authenticate as ephemeral remote OS users created by
1309	privileged authority. Used for normal remote work: running commands,
1310	file operations, interactive shells.
1311	
1312	Workload users are exclusive to one station user — not handed off between
1313	station users. Lifecycle is short:
1314	{busn_conscript}
1315	when needed,
1316	{busn_discharge}
1317	when done.
1318	
1319	=== Tabtarget Catalog
1320	
1321	Tabtargets under `buw-` consume {burn_regime} profiles to perform remote
1322	operations. Tier-specific colophons follow `buw-n[pw]` for clarity.
1323	
1324	==== Lifecycle
1325	
1326	`buw-rnl`::
1327	List all {burn_regime} profiles in the local station subtree.
1328	
1329	`buw-rnr <alias>`::
1330	Render a single {burn_regime} profile.
1331	
1332	`buw-rnv <alias>`::
1333	Validate a single {burn_regime} profile.
1334	
1335	==== Privileged (per-platform node setup)
1336	
1337	`buw-npg{l,m,c,w,p,x} <host> <user> <moniker>`::
1338	{busn_garrison} a node for the named platform.
1339	Writes the privileged {burn_regime} profile and runs the platform-specific
1340	ceremony to establish admin trust.
1341	Platform letters: l=Linux, m=Mac, c=Cygwin, w=WSL, p=PowerShell, x=Localhost.
1342	
1343	==== Workload (privileged authority required)
1344	
1345	`buw-nwc{l,m,c,w,p,x} <privileged-alias> <workload-moniker>`::
1346	{busn_conscript} a workload user via the named privileged BURN.
1347	Creates the remote OS user, installs the station user's pubkey, and
1348	writes a workload {burn_regime} profile locally.
1349	
1350	`buw-nwd <workload-alias>`::
1351	{busn_discharge} a workload user.
1352	Deletes the remote OS user via the workload profile's privileged
1353	authority and removes the local profile.
1354	
1355	`buw-nwi <privileged-alias>`::
1356	{busn_inventory} workload users present on the node, queried via the
1357	privileged BURN.
1358	
1359	==== Workload (operational)
1360	
1361	`buw-nwc <workload-alias>`::
1362	Check responsiveness via passwordless SSH probe.
1363	
1364	`buw-nwr <workload-alias> <cmd-file>`::
1365	Run the contents of `<cmd-file>` on the remote node; capture output to
1366	{burd_output_dir}.
1367	
1368	`buw-nws <workload-alias>`::
1369	Open an interactive SSH session.
1370	
1371	==== Handbook
1372	
1373	`buw-hn0`::
1374	Top-level handbook landing for node access procedures.
1375	
1376	=== Privileges
1377	
1378	The {burs_user} owns its profile subtree at `.buk/users/{burs_user}/`
1379	exclusively — operations write only under the current station user's tree.
1380	
1381	A {burn_regime} profile describes a connection identity (host, user, shell,
1382	alias, key, tier) — not project state, not work-directory. Project context
1383	is supplied by the consumer at invocation time.
1384	
1385	Each platform subdoc declares the remote-user privilege contract that its
1386	ceremony assumes (e.g., on Windows the privileged user must be a member of
1387	the Administrators group).
1388	
1389	=== Verb Definitions
1390	
1391	[[busn_garrison]]
1392	//axl_voices axt_command
1393	{busn_garrison}::
1394	Establish privileged BURN trust on a node by writing the privileged
1395	{burn_regime} profile and running the platform-specific ceremony to gain
1396	admin access. Per-platform tabtargets at `buw-npg{platform}`. First-time
1397	node setup; idempotent on re-run.
1398	
1399	[[busn_conscript]]
1400	//axl_voices axt_command
1401	{busn_conscript}::
1402	Create an ephemeral workload user on a node and provision a workload
1403	{burn_regime} profile locally. Requires a privileged BURN as authority for
1404	the remote-user creation. Per-platform tabtargets at `buw-nwc{platform}`,
1405	where the platform letter selects the workload shell — independent of the
1406	privileged BURN's shell choice.
1407	
1408	[[busn_discharge]]
1409	//axl_voices axt_command
1410	{busn_discharge}::
1411	Remove a workload user from a node and delete the local workload
1412	{burn_regime} profile. The workload profile encodes its privileged
1413	authority; {busn_discharge} uses that authority to delete the remote OS
1414	user. Tabtarget: `buw-nwd`.
1415	
1416	[[busn_inventory]]
1417	//axl_voices axt_command
1418	{busn_inventory}::
1419	List workload users currently provisioned on a node, queried via a
1420	privileged BURN. Scope is the node's full workload-user population — not
1421	just the current station user's history. Tabtarget: `buw-nwi`.
1422	
1423	include::BUSNW-NodeWindows.adoc[]
1424	
1425	include::BUSNL-NodeLinux.adoc[]
1426	
1427	include::BUSNM-NodeMac.adoc[]

file.content:
== Remote Node Access

Bringing remote nodes under SSH key control from a local station, establishing
trust that supports both privileged node-level operations and ephemeral
workload-tier work over the same {burn_regime}.

=== Privilege Tiers

The {burn_tier} field of every {burn_regime} profile classifies the profile
as either `privileged` or `workload`. Tabtargets refuse aliases of the wrong
tier at entry.

==== Privileged Tier

Privileged profiles authenticate as a remote OS user with admin or equivalent
privileges. Used for node-level operations: sshd configuration, user
provisioning, node-establishment ceremonies.

Multiple station users may hold privileged trust on the same node (file
format supports multi-resident keys). Operational convention: only one
station user actively orchestrates work at a time. This convention is
enforced by social contract, not BUK locking.

==== Workload Tier

Workload profiles authenticate as ephemeral remote OS users created by
privileged authority. Used for normal remote work: running commands,
file operations, interactive shells.

Workload users are exclusive to one station user — not handed off between
station users. Lifecycle is short:
{busn_conscript}
when needed,
{busn_discharge}
when done.

=== Tabtarget Catalog

Tabtargets under `buw-` consume {burn_regime} profiles to perform remote
operations. Tier-specific colophons follow `buw-n[pw]` for clarity.

==== Lifecycle

`buw-rnl`::
List all {burn_regime} profiles in the local station subtree.

`buw-rnr <alias>`::
Render a single {burn_regime} profile.

`buw-rnv <alias>`::
Validate a single {burn_regime} profile.

==== Privileged (per-platform node setup)

`buw-npg{l,m,c,w,p,x} <host> <user> <moniker>`::
{busn_garrison} a node for the named platform.
Writes the privileged {burn_regime} profile and runs the platform-specific
ceremony to establish admin trust.
Platform letters: l=Linux, m=Mac, c=Cygwin, w=WSL, p=PowerShell, x=Localhost.

==== Workload (privileged authority required)

`buw-nwc{l,m,c,w,p,x} <privileged-alias> <workload-moniker>`::
{busn_conscript} a workload user via the named privileged BURN.
Creates the remote OS user, installs the station user's pubkey, and
writes a workload {burn_regime} profile locally.

`buw-nwd <workload-alias>`::
{busn_discharge} a workload user.
Deletes the remote OS user via the workload profile's privileged
authority and removes the local profile.

`buw-nwi <privileged-alias>`::
{busn_inventory} workload users present on the node, queried via the
privileged BURN.

==== Workload (operational)

`buw-nwc <workload-alias>`::
Check responsiveness via passwordless SSH probe.

`buw-nwr <workload-alias> <cmd-file>`::
Run the contents of `<cmd-file>` on the remote node; capture output to
{burd_output_dir}.

`buw-nws <workload-alias>`::
Open an interactive SSH session.

==== Handbook

`buw-hn0`::
Top-level handbook landing for node access procedures.

=== Privileges

The {burs_user} owns its profile subtree at `.buk/users/{burs_user}/`
exclusively — operations write only under the current station user's tree.

A {burn_regime} profile describes a connection identity (host, user, shell,
alias, key, tier) — not project state, not work-directory. Project context
is supplied by the consumer at invocation time.

Each platform subdoc declares the remote-user privilege contract that its
ceremony assumes (e.g., on Windows the privileged user must be a member of
the Administrators group).

=== Verb Definitions

[[busn_garrison]]
//axl_voices axt_command
{busn_garrison}::
Establish privileged BURN trust on a node by writing the privileged
{burn_regime} profile and running the platform-specific ceremony to gain
admin access. Per-platform tabtargets at `buw-npg{platform}`. First-time
node setup; idempotent on re-run.

[[busn_conscript]]
//axl_voices axt_command
{busn_conscript}::
Create an ephemeral workload user on a node and provision a workload
{burn_regime} profile locally. Requires a privileged BURN as authority for
the remote-user creation. Per-platform tabtargets at `buw-nwc{platform}`,
where the platform letter selects the workload shell — independent of the
privileged BURN's shell choice.

[[busn_discharge]]
//axl_voices axt_command
{busn_discharge}::
Remove a workload user from a node and delete the local workload
{burn_regime} profile. The workload profile encodes its privileged
authority; {busn_discharge} uses that authority to delete the remote OS
user. Tabtarget: `buw-nwd`.

[[busn_inventory]]
//axl_voices axt_command
{busn_inventory}::
List workload users currently provisioned on a node, queried via a
privileged BURN. Scope is the node's full workload-user population — not
just the current station user's history. Tabtarget: `buw-nwi`.

include::BUSNW-NodeWindows.adoc[]

include::BUSNL-NodeLinux.adoc[]

include::BUSNM-NodeMac.adoc[]

1	=== Windows Node Access
2	
3	Platform-specific ceremony and contracts for Windows node access.
4	
5	==== Status
6	
7	Stub.
8	Elaborate content to be drafted by subsequent paces — Windows is the
9	canonical worked example for the Remote Node Access ceremony, requiring
10	detailed coverage of OpenSSH server provisioning, the
11	`administrators_authorized_keys` special case, ACL fixes via `icacls`,
12	and the temp-password bootstrap handshake.
13	
14	==== Privilege Contract
15	
16	The {burn_user} of a Windows privileged BURN must be a member of the local
17	Administrators group.
18	Rationale: Windows OpenSSH consults `C:\ProgramData\ssh\administrators_authorized_keys`
19	for users in the Administrators group, ignoring the per-user
20	`~/.ssh/authorized_keys` for those users.
21	A non-admin path would require parallel implementation with no return for
22	the supported use cases.
23	
24	Asserted at every ceremony participant before any modification.
25	
26	==== Platform Letters
27	
28	* `c` — Cygwin (`buw-npgc`, `buw-nwcc`)
29	* `w` — WSL (`buw-npgw`, `buw-nwcw`)
30	* `p` — PowerShell (`buw-npgp`, `buw-nwcp`)
31	
32	==== Out of Band
33	
34	The console phase of the Windows ceremony is performed at the Windows
35	physical keyboard (or RDP) by the operator and is described in the BUK
36	handbook surface, not in this spec.
37

=== Windows Node Access

Platform-specific ceremony and contracts for Windows node access.

==== Status

Stub.
Elaborate content to be drafted by subsequent paces — Windows is the
canonical worked example for the Remote Node Access ceremony, requiring
detailed coverage of OpenSSH server provisioning, the
`administrators_authorized_keys` special case, ACL fixes via `icacls`,
and the temp-password bootstrap handshake.

==== Privilege Contract

The {burn_user} of a Windows privileged BURN must be a member of the local
Administrators group.
Rationale: Windows OpenSSH consults `C:\ProgramData\ssh\administrators_authorized_keys`
for users in the Administrators group, ignoring the per-user
`~/.ssh/authorized_keys` for those users.
A non-admin path would require parallel implementation with no return for
the supported use cases.

Asserted at every ceremony participant before any modification.

==== Platform Letters

* `c` — Cygwin (`buw-npgc`, `buw-nwcc`)
* `w` — WSL (`buw-npgw`, `buw-nwcw`)
* `p` — PowerShell (`buw-npgp`, `buw-nwcp`)

==== Out of Band

The console phase of the Windows ceremony is performed at the Windows
physical keyboard (or RDP) by the operator and is described in the BUK
handbook surface, not in this spec.

1	#!/bin/bash
2	#
3	# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
4	#
5	# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
6	# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
7	# You may obtain a copy of the License at
8	#
9	#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
10	#
11	# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
12	# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
13	# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
14	# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
15	# limitations under the License.
16	#
17	# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
18	#
19	# BUW Zipper - Colophon registry for BUK workbench dispatch
20	
21	set -euo pipefail
22	
23	# Multiple inclusion guard
24	test -z "${ZBUWZ_SOURCED:-}" || return 0
25	ZBUWZ_SOURCED=1
26	
27	######################################################################
28	# Colophon registry initialization
29	
30	zbuwz_kindle() {
31	  test -z "${ZBUWZ_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "buwz already kindled"
32	
33	  # Verify buz zipper is kindled (CLI furnish must kindle buz first)
34	  zbuz_sentinel
35	
36	  # TabTarget subsystem (buut_cli.sh)
37	  local z_mod="buut_cli.sh"
38	  buz_enroll BUWZ_TT_LIST_LAUNCHERS      "buw-tt-ll"  "${z_mod}" "buut_list_launchers"               ""  "List all registered launchers"
39	  buz_enroll BUWZ_TT_BATCH_LOGGING       "buw-tt-cbl" "${z_mod}" "buut_tabtarget_batch_logging"      ""  "Create batch tabtarget with logging"
40	  buz_enroll BUWZ_TT_BATCH_NOLOG         "buw-tt-cbn" "${z_mod}" "buut_tabtarget_batch_nolog"        ""  "Create batch tabtarget without logging"
41	  buz_enroll BUWZ_TT_INTERACTIVE_LOGGING "buw-tt-cil" "${z_mod}" "buut_tabtarget_interactive_logging" ""  "Create interactive tabtarget with logging"
42	  buz_enroll BUWZ_TT_INTERACTIVE_NOLOG   "buw-tt-cin" "${z_mod}" "buut_tabtarget_interactive_nolog"  ""  "Create interactive tabtarget without logging"
43	  buz_enroll BUWZ_TT_LAUNCHER            "buw-tt-cl"  "${z_mod}" "buut_launcher"                     ""  "Create launcher script"
44	
45	  # Config Regime subsystem (burc_cli.sh)
46	  z_mod="burc_cli.sh"
47	  buz_enroll BUWZ_RC_VALIDATE "buw-rcv" "${z_mod}" "burc_validate"  ""  "Validate BURC regime"
48	  buz_enroll BUWZ_RC_RENDER   "buw-rcr" "${z_mod}" "burc_render"    ""  "Render BURC regime"
49	
50	  # Station Regime subsystem (burs_cli.sh)
51	  z_mod="burs_cli.sh"
52	  buz_enroll BUWZ_RS_VALIDATE "buw-rsv" "${z_mod}" "burs_validate"  ""  "Validate BURS regime"
53	  buz_enroll BUWZ_RS_RENDER   "buw-rsr" "${z_mod}" "burs_render"    ""  "Render BURS regime"
54	
55	  # Environment Regime subsystem (bure_cli.sh)
56	  z_mod="bure_cli.sh"
57	  buz_enroll BUWZ_RE_VALIDATE "buw-rev" "${z_mod}" "bure_validate"  ""  "Validate BURE regime"
58	  buz_enroll BUWZ_RE_RENDER   "buw-rer" "${z_mod}" "bure_render"    ""  "Render BURE regime"
59	
60	  # Host Regime subsystem (burh_cli.sh)
61	  z_mod="burh_cli.sh"
62	  buz_enroll BUWZ_RH_VALIDATE "buw-rhv" "${z_mod}" "burh_validate" "param1" "Validate BURH profile"
63	  buz_enroll BUWZ_RH_RENDER   "buw-rhr" "${z_mod}" "burh_render"   "param1" "Render BURH profile"
64	  buz_enroll BUWZ_RH_LIST     "buw-rhl" "${z_mod}" "burh_list"     ""       "List BURH profiles"
65	
66	  # Qualification subsystem (buq_cli.sh)
67	  z_mod="buq_cli.sh"
68	  buz_enroll BUWZ_QUALIFY_SHELLCHECK "buw-qsc" "${z_mod}" "buq_shellcheck"  ""  "Run shellcheck on all tools"
69	
70	  # Test fixtures (bux_cli.sh)
71	  z_mod="bux_cli.sh"
72	  buz_enroll BUWZ_DELAY "buw-xd" "${z_mod}" "bux_delay"  ""  "Sleep 20 seconds (timing fixture)"
73	
74	  # Self-test (butt_testbench.sh)
75	  z_mod="butt_testbench.sh"
76	  buz_enroll BUWZ_SELF_TEST "buw-st" "${z_mod}" "buw-st"  ""  "BUK self-test (kick-tires + bure-tweak)"
77	
78	  # Host Regime — constructors + SSH automation (burh_cli.sh)
79	  z_mod="burh_cli.sh"
80	  buz_enroll BUWZ_RHC_LINUX      "buw-rhcl"  "${z_mod}" "burh_construct_linux"      ""        "Construct BURH profile: Linux"
81	  buz_enroll BUWZ_RHC_MAC        "buw-rhcm"  "${z_mod}" "burh_construct_mac"        ""        "Construct BURH profile: macOS"
82	  buz_enroll BUWZ_RHC_CYGWIN     "buw-rhcc"  "${z_mod}" "burh_construct_cygwin"     ""        "Construct BURH profile: Cygwin"
83	  buz_enroll BUWZ_RHC_WSL        "buw-rhcw"  "${z_mod}" "burh_construct_wsl"        ""        "Construct BURH profile: WSL"
84	  buz_enroll BUWZ_RHC_POWERSHELL "buw-rhcp"  "${z_mod}" "burh_construct_powershell" ""        "Construct BURH profile: PowerShell"
85	  buz_enroll BUWZ_RHC_LOCALHOST  "buw-rhcx"  "${z_mod}" "burh_construct_localhost"  ""        "Construct BURH profile: localhost"
86	  buz_enroll BUWZ_HW_SSH_CONFIG  "buw-HWsc"  "${z_mod}" "burh_ssh_config"           ""        "Write SSH config from BURH profiles"
87	  buz_enroll BUWZ_HW_VERIFY_SSH  "buw-HWvs"  "${z_mod}" "burh_verify_ssh"           "param1"  "Verify SSH to BURH profile"
88	  buz_enroll BUWZ_RH_INSTALL_KEY "buw-rhk"   "${z_mod}" "burh_install_key"          "param1"  "Install BURH key into authorized_keys"
89	
90	  # Windows Commands (buwc_cli.sh)
91	  z_mod="buwc_cli.sh"
92	  buz_enroll BUWZ_WC_BOOTSTRAP   "buw-wcb"   "${z_mod}" "buwc_bootstrap_sshd"       ""        "Provision sshd on Windows (WSL)"
93	  buz_enroll BUWZ_WC_INSTALL_KEY "buw-wck"   "${z_mod}" "buwc_install_key"          "param1"  "Install BURH key into Windows admin authorized_keys"
94	
95	  # Handbook — top index + Windows OS procedures (buhw_cli.sh)
96	  z_mod="buhw_cli.sh"
97	  buz_enroll BUWZ_H0_TOP            "buw-h0"    "${z_mod}" "buhw_handbook_top"        ""        "BUK top-level handbook index"
98	  buz_enroll BUWZ_HW_TOP            "buw-hw"    "${z_mod}" "buhw_top"                ""        "BUK-level Windows procedures checklist"
99	  buz_enroll BUWZ_HW_ACCESS_BASE    "buw-HWab"  "${z_mod}" "buhw_access_base"        ""        "OpenSSH server install + lockdown"
100	  buz_enroll BUWZ_HW_ACCESS_REMOTE  "buw-HWar"  "${z_mod}" "buhw_access_remote"      ""        "Client key gen + ssh config"
101	  buz_enroll BUWZ_HW_ACCESS_ENTRY   "buw-HWax"  "${z_mod}" "buhw_access_entrypoints" ""        "SSH command= routing + icacls"
102	  buz_enroll BUWZ_HW_ENV_WSL        "buw-HWew"  "${z_mod}" "buhw_environment_wsl"    "param1"  "WSL distro creation"
103	  buz_enroll BUWZ_HW_ENV_CYGWIN     "buw-HWec"  "${z_mod}" "buhw_environment_cygwin" ""        "Cygwin install"
104	
105	  readonly ZBUWZ_KINDLED=1
106	}
107	
108	######################################################################
109	# Healthcheck (validates all enrolled tabtargets exist on disk)
110	
111	zbuwz_healthcheck() {
112	  zbuwz_sentinel
113	  buz_healthcheck
114	}
115	
116	######################################################################
117	# Internal sentinel
118	
119	zbuwz_sentinel() {
120	  test "${ZBUWZ_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module buwz not kindled - call zbuwz_kindle first"
121	}
122	
123	# eof
124

# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# BUW Zipper - Colophon registry for BUK workbench dispatch

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion guard
test -z "${ZBUWZ_SOURCED:-}" || return 0
ZBUWZ_SOURCED=1

######################################################################
# Colophon registry initialization

zbuwz_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZBUWZ_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "buwz already kindled"

  # Verify buz zipper is kindled (CLI furnish must kindle buz first)
  zbuz_sentinel

  # TabTarget subsystem (buut_cli.sh)
  local z_mod="buut_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_TT_LIST_LAUNCHERS      "buw-tt-ll"  "${z_mod}" "buut_list_launchers"               ""  "List all registered launchers"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_TT_BATCH_LOGGING       "buw-tt-cbl" "${z_mod}" "buut_tabtarget_batch_logging"      ""  "Create batch tabtarget with logging"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_TT_BATCH_NOLOG         "buw-tt-cbn" "${z_mod}" "buut_tabtarget_batch_nolog"        ""  "Create batch tabtarget without logging"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_TT_INTERACTIVE_LOGGING "buw-tt-cil" "${z_mod}" "buut_tabtarget_interactive_logging" ""  "Create interactive tabtarget with logging"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_TT_INTERACTIVE_NOLOG   "buw-tt-cin" "${z_mod}" "buut_tabtarget_interactive_nolog"  ""  "Create interactive tabtarget without logging"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_TT_LAUNCHER            "buw-tt-cl"  "${z_mod}" "buut_launcher"                     ""  "Create launcher script"

  # Config Regime subsystem (burc_cli.sh)
  z_mod="burc_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RC_VALIDATE "buw-rcv" "${z_mod}" "burc_validate"  ""  "Validate BURC regime"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RC_RENDER   "buw-rcr" "${z_mod}" "burc_render"    ""  "Render BURC regime"

  # Station Regime subsystem (burs_cli.sh)
  z_mod="burs_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RS_VALIDATE "buw-rsv" "${z_mod}" "burs_validate"  ""  "Validate BURS regime"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RS_RENDER   "buw-rsr" "${z_mod}" "burs_render"    ""  "Render BURS regime"

  # Environment Regime subsystem (bure_cli.sh)
  z_mod="bure_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RE_VALIDATE "buw-rev" "${z_mod}" "bure_validate"  ""  "Validate BURE regime"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RE_RENDER   "buw-rer" "${z_mod}" "bure_render"    ""  "Render BURE regime"

  # Host Regime subsystem (burh_cli.sh)
  z_mod="burh_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RH_VALIDATE "buw-rhv" "${z_mod}" "burh_validate" "param1" "Validate BURH profile"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RH_RENDER   "buw-rhr" "${z_mod}" "burh_render"   "param1" "Render BURH profile"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RH_LIST     "buw-rhl" "${z_mod}" "burh_list"     ""       "List BURH profiles"

  # Qualification subsystem (buq_cli.sh)
  z_mod="buq_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_QUALIFY_SHELLCHECK "buw-qsc" "${z_mod}" "buq_shellcheck"  ""  "Run shellcheck on all tools"

  # Test fixtures (bux_cli.sh)
  z_mod="bux_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_DELAY "buw-xd" "${z_mod}" "bux_delay"  ""  "Sleep 20 seconds (timing fixture)"

  # Self-test (butt_testbench.sh)
  z_mod="butt_testbench.sh"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_SELF_TEST "buw-st" "${z_mod}" "buw-st"  ""  "BUK self-test (kick-tires + bure-tweak)"

  # Host Regime — constructors + SSH automation (burh_cli.sh)
  z_mod="burh_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RHC_LINUX      "buw-rhcl"  "${z_mod}" "burh_construct_linux"      ""        "Construct BURH profile: Linux"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RHC_MAC        "buw-rhcm"  "${z_mod}" "burh_construct_mac"        ""        "Construct BURH profile: macOS"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RHC_CYGWIN     "buw-rhcc"  "${z_mod}" "burh_construct_cygwin"     ""        "Construct BURH profile: Cygwin"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RHC_WSL        "buw-rhcw"  "${z_mod}" "burh_construct_wsl"        ""        "Construct BURH profile: WSL"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RHC_POWERSHELL "buw-rhcp"  "${z_mod}" "burh_construct_powershell" ""        "Construct BURH profile: PowerShell"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RHC_LOCALHOST  "buw-rhcx"  "${z_mod}" "burh_construct_localhost"  ""        "Construct BURH profile: localhost"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_HW_SSH_CONFIG  "buw-HWsc"  "${z_mod}" "burh_ssh_config"           ""        "Write SSH config from BURH profiles"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_HW_VERIFY_SSH  "buw-HWvs"  "${z_mod}" "burh_verify_ssh"           "param1"  "Verify SSH to BURH profile"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RH_INSTALL_KEY "buw-rhk"   "${z_mod}" "burh_install_key"          "param1"  "Install BURH key into authorized_keys"

  # Windows Commands (buwc_cli.sh)
  z_mod="buwc_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_WC_BOOTSTRAP   "buw-wcb"   "${z_mod}" "buwc_bootstrap_sshd"       ""        "Provision sshd on Windows (WSL)"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_WC_INSTALL_KEY "buw-wck"   "${z_mod}" "buwc_install_key"          "param1"  "Install BURH key into Windows admin authorized_keys"

  # Handbook — top index + Windows OS procedures (buhw_cli.sh)
  z_mod="buhw_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_H0_TOP            "buw-h0"    "${z_mod}" "buhw_handbook_top"        ""        "BUK top-level handbook index"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_HW_TOP            "buw-hw"    "${z_mod}" "buhw_top"                ""        "BUK-level Windows procedures checklist"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_HW_ACCESS_BASE    "buw-HWab"  "${z_mod}" "buhw_access_base"        ""        "OpenSSH server install + lockdown"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_HW_ACCESS_REMOTE  "buw-HWar"  "${z_mod}" "buhw_access_remote"      ""        "Client key gen + ssh config"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_HW_ACCESS_ENTRY   "buw-HWax"  "${z_mod}" "buhw_access_entrypoints" ""        "SSH command= routing + icacls"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_HW_ENV_WSL        "buw-HWew"  "${z_mod}" "buhw_environment_wsl"    "param1"  "WSL distro creation"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_HW_ENV_CYGWIN     "buw-HWec"  "${z_mod}" "buhw_environment_cygwin" ""        "Cygwin install"

  readonly ZBUWZ_KINDLED=1
}

######################################################################
# Healthcheck (validates all enrolled tabtargets exist on disk)

zbuwz_healthcheck() {
  zbuwz_sentinel
  buz_healthcheck
}

######################################################################
# Internal sentinel

zbuwz_sentinel() {
  test "${ZBUWZ_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module buwz not kindled - call zbuwz_kindle first"
}

# eof

15	# limitations under the License.
16	#
17	# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
18	#
19	# BURH Regime - Bash Utility Regime Host Module
20	#
21	# BURH is a multi-instance regime — one file per SSH connection profile.
22	# Each profile lives at .buk/users/${BURS_USER}/<alias>/burh.env
23	
24	set -euo pipefail
25	
26	# Multiple inclusion detection
27	test -z "${ZBURH_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module burh multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
28	ZBURH_SOURCED=1

30	######################################################################
31	# Internal Functions (zburh_*)
32	
33	zburh_kindle() {
34	  test -z "${ZBURH_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module burh already kindled"
35	
36	  # No defaults set — buv uses ${!varname:-} for safe indirect expansion under set -u.
37	  # Unset variables are detected distinctly from empty by zbuv_check_capture.
38	
39	  # Enroll all BURH variables — single source of truth for validation and rendering
40	
41	  buv_regime_enroll BURH
42	
43	  buv_group_enroll "Connection Identity"
44	  buv_string_enroll  BURH_HOST        1  253  "IP address or hostname of the remote machine"
45	  buv_xname_enroll   BURH_USER        1   32  "Username on the remote host"
46	  buv_xname_enroll   BURH_ALIAS       1   64  "SSH alias and key filename (must match directory name)"
47	
48	  buv_group_enroll "Authentication"
49	  buv_string_enroll  BURH_SSH_PUBKEY  20 1024  "Full public key line (e.g., ssh-ed25519 AAAA... user@host)"
50	  buv_string_enroll  BURH_KEY_FILE     0  256  "SSH private key filename in ~/.ssh/ (empty defaults to alias)"
51	
52	  buv_group_enroll "Session Routing"
53	  buv_string_enroll  BURH_COMMAND      0  512  "Shell command for command= routing in authorized_keys (empty for direct shell)"
54	
55	  # Guard against unexpected BURH_ variables not in enrollment
56	  buv_scope_sentinel BURH BURH_
57	
58	  # Lock all enrolled BURH_ variables against mutation
59	  buv_lock BURH
60	
61	  readonly ZBURH_KINDLED=1
62	}
63	
64	zburh_sentinel() {
65	  test "${ZBURH_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module burh not kindled - call zburh_kindle first"
66	}
67	
68	# Enforce all BURH enrollment validations
69	zburh_enforce() {
70	  zburh_sentinel
71	
72	  buv_vet BURH
73	}
74	
75	######################################################################
76	# Public Functions (burh_*)
77	
78	# Build a BURH authorized_keys line with alias marker.
79	# Pure string builder — no I/O. Caller decides how to write.
80	# Sets ZBURH_KEY_LINE (the full line) and ZBURH_KEY_MARKER (the grep tag).
81	# Prerequisite: BURH kindled (needs BURH_ALIAS, BURH_COMMAND, BURH_SSH_PUBKEY)
82	zburh_build_key_line() {
83	  zburh_sentinel
84	
85	  ZBURH_KEY_MARKER="# BURH:${BURH_ALIAS}"
86	
87	  if test -n "${BURH_COMMAND}"; then
88	    ZBURH_KEY_LINE="command=\"${BURH_COMMAND}\" ${BURH_SSH_PUBKEY} ${ZBURH_KEY_MARKER}"
89	  else
90	    ZBURH_KEY_LINE="${BURH_SSH_PUBKEY} ${ZBURH_KEY_MARKER}"
91	  fi
92	}
93	
94	# List available alias names as space-separated tokens.
95	# Emits one <alias>.${BUF_EXT_ALIAS} observation fact per profile (empty
96	# content; presence is the fact).
97	# Prerequisite: BURS kindled (needs BURS_USER), BURD kindled (needs BURD_CONFIG_DIR)
98	burh_list_capture() {
99	  zburs_sentinel
100	
101	  local -r z_user_dir="${BURD_CONFIG_DIR}/users/${BURS_USER}"
102	  test -d "${z_user_dir}" || return 1
103	
104	  local z_result=""
105	  local z_files=("${z_user_dir}"/*/burh.env)
106	  local z_i=""
107	  for z_i in "${!z_files[@]}"; do
108	    test -f "${z_files[$z_i]}" || continue
109	    local z_dir="${z_files[$z_i]%/*}"
110	    local z_alias="${z_dir##*/}"
111	    buf_write_fact_multi "${z_alias}" "${BUF_EXT_ALIAS}" ""
112	    z_result="${z_result}${z_result:+ }${z_alias}"
113	  done
114	  test -n "${z_result}" || return 1
115	  echo "${z_result}"
116	}
117

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# BURH Regime - Bash Utility Regime Host Module
#
# BURH is a multi-instance regime — one file per SSH connection profile.
# Each profile lives at .buk/users/${BURS_USER}/<alias>/burh.env

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion detection
test -z "${ZBURH_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module burh multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
ZBURH_SOURCED=1

######################################################################
# Internal Functions (zburh_*)

zburh_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZBURH_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module burh already kindled"

  # No defaults set — buv uses ${!varname:-} for safe indirect expansion under set -u.
  # Unset variables are detected distinctly from empty by zbuv_check_capture.

  # Enroll all BURH variables — single source of truth for validation and rendering

  buv_regime_enroll BURH

  buv_group_enroll "Connection Identity"
  buv_string_enroll  BURH_HOST        1  253  "IP address or hostname of the remote machine"
  buv_xname_enroll   BURH_USER        1   32  "Username on the remote host"
  buv_xname_enroll   BURH_ALIAS       1   64  "SSH alias and key filename (must match directory name)"

  buv_group_enroll "Authentication"
  buv_string_enroll  BURH_SSH_PUBKEY  20 1024  "Full public key line (e.g., ssh-ed25519 AAAA... user@host)"
  buv_string_enroll  BURH_KEY_FILE     0  256  "SSH private key filename in ~/.ssh/ (empty defaults to alias)"

  buv_group_enroll "Session Routing"
  buv_string_enroll  BURH_COMMAND      0  512  "Shell command for command= routing in authorized_keys (empty for direct shell)"

  # Guard against unexpected BURH_ variables not in enrollment
  buv_scope_sentinel BURH BURH_

  # Lock all enrolled BURH_ variables against mutation
  buv_lock BURH

  readonly ZBURH_KINDLED=1
}

zburh_sentinel() {
  test "${ZBURH_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module burh not kindled - call zburh_kindle first"
}

# Enforce all BURH enrollment validations
zburh_enforce() {
  zburh_sentinel

  buv_vet BURH
}

######################################################################
# Public Functions (burh_*)

# Build a BURH authorized_keys line with alias marker.
# Pure string builder — no I/O. Caller decides how to write.
# Sets ZBURH_KEY_LINE (the full line) and ZBURH_KEY_MARKER (the grep tag).
# Prerequisite: BURH kindled (needs BURH_ALIAS, BURH_COMMAND, BURH_SSH_PUBKEY)
zburh_build_key_line() {
  zburh_sentinel

  ZBURH_KEY_MARKER="# BURH:${BURH_ALIAS}"

  if test -n "${BURH_COMMAND}"; then
    ZBURH_KEY_LINE="command=\"${BURH_COMMAND}\" ${BURH_SSH_PUBKEY} ${ZBURH_KEY_MARKER}"
  else
    ZBURH_KEY_LINE="${BURH_SSH_PUBKEY} ${ZBURH_KEY_MARKER}"
  fi
}

# List available alias names as space-separated tokens.
# Emits one <alias>.${BUF_EXT_ALIAS} observation fact per profile (empty
# content; presence is the fact).
# Prerequisite: BURS kindled (needs BURS_USER), BURD kindled (needs BURD_CONFIG_DIR)
burh_list_capture() {
  zburs_sentinel

  local -r z_user_dir="${BURD_CONFIG_DIR}/users/${BURS_USER}"
  test -d "${z_user_dir}" || return 1

  local z_result=""
  local z_files=("${z_user_dir}"/*/burh.env)
  local z_i=""
  for z_i in "${!z_files[@]}"; do
    test -f "${z_files[$z_i]}" || continue
    local z_dir="${z_files[$z_i]%/*}"
    local z_alias="${z_dir##*/}"
    buf_write_fact_multi "${z_alias}" "${BUF_EXT_ALIAS}" ""
    z_result="${z_result}${z_result:+ }${z_alias}"
  done
  test -n "${z_result}" || return 1
  echo "${z_result}"
}

# eof

1	#!/bin/bash
2	#
3	# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
4	#
5	# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
6	# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
7	# You may obtain a copy of the License at
8	#
9	#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
10	#
11	# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
12	# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
13	# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
14	# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
15	# limitations under the License.
16	#
17	# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
18	#
19	# BURH CLI - Command line interface for BURH host regime operations
20	#
21	# Four command families:
22	#   burh_validate/render/list  — regime read operations (require BUZ_FOLIO)
23	#   burh_construct_*           — profile constructors (write burh.env)
24	#   burh_ssh_*/burh_verify_*   — SSH automation (config, verify)
25	#   burh_install_key           — authorized_keys management (per-profile, idempotent)
26	
27	set -euo pipefail
28	
29	source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buc_command.sh"
30	
31	######################################################################
32	# Command Functions — Regime Operations
33	
34	burh_validate() {
35	  buc_doc_brief "Validate BURH host regime profile via enrollment report"
36	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
37	
38	  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || buc_die "Profile alias required (e.g., winhost-cyg)"
39	  buc_step "Validating BURH host regime"
40	  buv_report BURH "Host Regime"
41	  buc_step "BURH host regime valid"
42	}
43	
44	burh_render() {
45	  buc_doc_brief "Display diagnostic view of BURH host regime profile"
46	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
47	
48	  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || buc_die "Profile alias required (e.g., winhost-cyg)"
49	  buv_render BURH "BURH - Bash Utility Host Regime"
50	}
51	
52	burh_list() {
53	  buc_doc_brief "List available BURH host profiles for current user"
54	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
55	
56	  local z_aliases
57	  z_aliases=$(burh_list_capture) || buc_die "No BURH profiles found for user: ${BURS_USER}"
58	  buc_step "Available profiles for ${BURS_USER}:"
59	  local z_alias=""
60	  for z_alias in ${z_aliases}; do
61	    buc_bare "        ${z_alias}"
62	  done
63	}
64	
65	######################################################################
66	# Internal: Constructor Helper
67	#
68	# Shared logic for all platform constructors.
69	# Args: host user moniker suffix command_value key_file
70	# command_value and key_file may be empty.
71	
72	zburh_construct() {
73	  local z_host="${1:-}"
74	  local z_user="${2:-}"
75	  local z_moniker="${3:-}"
76	  local z_suffix="${4:-}"
77	  local z_command="${5:-}"
78	  local z_key_file="${6:-}"
79	
80	  test -n "${z_host}"    || buc_die "host required (arg 1)"
81	  test -n "${z_user}"    || buc_die "user required (arg 2)"
82	  test -n "${z_moniker}" || buc_die "moniker required (arg 3)"
83	
84	  local -r z_alias="${z_moniker}-${z_suffix}"
85	  local -r z_profile_dir="${BURD_CONFIG_DIR}/users/${BURS_USER}/${z_alias}"
86	  local -r z_profile_file="${z_profile_dir}/burh.env"
87	  local -r z_pubkey_file="${HOME}/.ssh/${z_alias}.pub"
88	  local z_pubkey=""
89	  local z_has_pubkey=0
90	
91	  if test -f "${z_pubkey_file}"; then
92	    z_pubkey=$(head -1 "${z_pubkey_file}")
93	    z_has_pubkey=1
94	    buc_step "Found public key: ${z_pubkey_file}"
95	  else
96	    z_pubkey="ssh-ed25519 REPLACE_WITH_ACTUAL_PUBKEY ${BURS_USER}@$(hostname -s)"
97	  fi
98	
99	  mkdir -p "${z_profile_dir}"
100	
101	  printf '%s\n' "BURH_HOST=${z_host}"           >  "${z_profile_file}"
102	  printf '%s\n' "BURH_USER=${z_user}"            >> "${z_profile_file}"
103	  printf '%s\n' "BURH_ALIAS=${z_alias}"          >> "${z_profile_file}"
104	  printf '%s\n' "BURH_SSH_PUBKEY='${z_pubkey}'"  >> "${z_profile_file}"
105	  printf '%s\n' "BURH_KEY_FILE=${z_key_file}"    >> "${z_profile_file}"
106	  printf '%s\n' "BURH_COMMAND='${z_command}'"    >> "${z_profile_file}"
107	
108	  buc_step "BURH profile written: ${z_profile_file}"
109	
110	  if test "${z_has_pubkey}" = "0"; then
111	    buh_e
112	    buh_section "SSH Key Required"
113	    buyy_cmd_yawp "${z_pubkey_file}"; local -r z_pubkey_yelp="${z_buym_yelp}"
114	    buh_line "No public key at ${z_pubkey_yelp}."
115	    buh_line   "Generate one:"
116	    buh_e
117	    buh_code   "ssh-keygen -t ed25519 -f ~/.ssh/${z_alias}"
118	    buh_e
119	    buh_line   "Then re-run this constructor to populate the public key."
120	  fi
121	}
122	
123	######################################################################
124	# Command Functions — Constructors
125	
126	burh_construct_linux() {
127	  buc_doc_brief "Construct BURH profile for Linux target"
128	  buc_doc_param "host" "IP or hostname of the Linux machine"
129	  buc_doc_param "user" "Username on the remote host"
130	  buc_doc_param "moniker" "Short name (alias = moniker-linux)"
131	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
132	
133	  zburh_construct "${1:-}" "${2:-}" "${3:-}" "linux" ""
134	}
135	
136	burh_construct_mac() {
137	  buc_doc_brief "Construct BURH profile for macOS target"
138	  buc_doc_param "host" "IP or hostname of the Mac"
139	  buc_doc_param "user" "Username on the remote host"
140	  buc_doc_param "moniker" "Short name (alias = moniker-mac)"
141	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
142	
143	  zburh_construct "${1:-}" "${2:-}" "${3:-}" "mac" ""
144	}
145	
146	burh_construct_cygwin() {
147	  buc_doc_brief "Construct BURH profile for Windows Cygwin target"
148	  buc_doc_param "host" "IP or hostname of the Windows machine"
149	  buc_doc_param "user" "Username on the Windows host"
150	  buc_doc_param "moniker" "Short name (alias = moniker-cyg)"
151	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
152	
153	  zburh_construct "${1:-}" "${2:-}" "${3:-}" "cyg" 'C:\cygwin64\bin\bash.exe -l'
154	}
155	
156	burh_construct_wsl() {
157	  buc_doc_brief "Construct BURH profile for Windows WSL target"
158	  buc_doc_param "host" "IP or hostname of the Windows machine"
159	  buc_doc_param "user" "Username on the Windows host"
160	  buc_doc_param "moniker" "Short name (alias = moniker-wsl)"
161	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
162	
163	  local -r z_wsl_distro="Ubuntu"
164	  zburh_construct "${1:-}" "${2:-}" "${3:-}" "wsl" "C:\\Windows\\System32\\wsl.exe -d ${z_wsl_distro}"
165	}
166	
167	burh_construct_powershell() {
168	  buc_doc_brief "Construct BURH profile for Windows PowerShell target"
169	  buc_doc_param "host" "IP or hostname of the Windows machine"
170	  buc_doc_param "user" "Username on the Windows host"
171	  buc_doc_param "moniker" "Short name (alias = moniker-ps)"
172	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
173	
174	  zburh_construct "${1:-}" "${2:-}" "${3:-}" "ps" 'C:\Windows\System32\WindowsPowerShell\v1.0\powershell.exe'
175	}
176	
177	burh_construct_localhost() {
178	  buc_doc_brief "Construct BURH profile for localhost (local SSH)"
179	  buc_doc_param "user" "Username on this machine"
180	  buc_doc_param "moniker" "Short name (alias = moniker-local)"
181	  buc_doc_shown || return 0

184	}
185	
186	######################################################################
187	# Command Functions — SSH Automation
188	
189	burh_ssh_config() {
190	  buc_doc_brief "Generate SSH config entries from all BURH profiles"
191	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
192	
193	  local z_aliases
194	  z_aliases=$(burh_list_capture) || buc_die "No BURH profiles found for user: ${BURS_USER}"
195	
196	  local -r z_config="${HOME}/.ssh/config"
197	  local -r z_begin="# --- BEGIN BURH MANAGED ---"
198	  local -r z_end="# --- END BURH MANAGED ---"
199	
200	  # Build managed section content
201	  local z_section=""
202	  z_section="${z_begin}"$'\n'
203	  z_section+="# Generated by buw-HWsc — do not edit this section manually"$'\n'
204	
205	  local z_alias=""
206	  for z_alias in ${z_aliases}; do
207	    local z_profile="${BURD_CONFIG_DIR}/users/${BURS_USER}/${z_alias}/burh.env"
208	    # Source directly — BURH not kindled, no readonly conflict
209	    source "${z_profile}"
210	    z_section+=$'\n'
211	    local z_key="${BURH_KEY_FILE:-${BURH_ALIAS}}"
212	    z_section+="Host ${BURH_ALIAS}"$'\n'
213	    z_section+="  HostName ${BURH_HOST}"$'\n'
214	    z_section+="  User ${BURH_USER}"$'\n'
215	    z_section+="  IdentityFile ~/.ssh/${z_key}"$'\n'
216	  done
217	
218	  z_section+="${z_end}"$'\n'
219	
220	  # Write managed section into config
221	  mkdir -p "${HOME}/.ssh"
222	
223	  if test -f "${z_config}" && grep -q "${z_begin}" "${z_config}"; then
224	    # Replace existing managed section
225	    local -r z_temp=$(mktemp)
226	    local z_in_managed=0
227	    while IFS= read -r z_line || test -n "${z_line}"; do
228	      if test "${z_line}" = "${z_begin}"; then
229	        z_in_managed=1
230	        printf '%s' "${z_section}" >> "${z_temp}"
231	        continue
232	      fi
233	      if test "${z_in_managed}" = "1"; then
234	        test "${z_line}" = "${z_end}" && z_in_managed=0
235	        continue
236	      fi
237	      printf '%s\n' "${z_line}" >> "${z_temp}"
238	    done < "${z_config}"
239	    mv "${z_temp}" "${z_config}"
240	  elif test -f "${z_config}"; then
241	    # Append managed section
242	    printf '\n%s' "${z_section}" >> "${z_config}"
243	  else
244	    # Create new config
245	    printf '%s' "${z_section}" > "${z_config}"
246	  fi
247	
248	  chmod 600 "${z_config}"
249	  buc_step "SSH config updated: ${z_config}"
250	  buc_step "Managed profiles: ${z_aliases}"
251	}
252	
253	burh_verify_ssh() {
254	  buc_doc_brief "Verify SSH connectivity to a BURH profile"
255	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
256	
257	  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || buc_die "Profile alias required (e.g., winhost-cyg)"
258	
259	  buc_step "Testing SSH to ${BURH_ALIAS} (${BURH_HOST})..."
260	  local z_result=""
261	  if z_result=$(ssh -o BatchMode=yes -o ConnectTimeout=10 "${BURH_ALIAS}" whoami 2>&1); then
262	    buc_success "SSH OK: ${BURH_ALIAS} -> ${z_result}"
263	  else
264	    buc_die "SSH FAILED: ${BURH_ALIAS} -> ${z_result}"
265	  fi
266	}
267	
268	burh_install_key() {
269	  buc_doc_brief "Install BURH profile SSH key into authorized_keys (idempotent)"
270	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
271	
272	  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || buc_die "Profile alias required (e.g., winhost-cyg)"
273	
274	  local -r z_auth_keys="${HOME}/.ssh/authorized_keys"
275	
276	  zburh_build_key_line
277	
278	  mkdir -p "${HOME}/.ssh"
279	
280	  if test -f "${z_auth_keys}"; then
281	    local -r z_temp=$(mktemp)
282	    grep -v "${ZBURH_KEY_MARKER}" "${z_auth_keys}" > "${z_temp}" || true
283	    mv "${z_temp}" "${z_auth_keys}"
284	  fi
285	
286	  printf '%s\n' "${ZBURH_KEY_LINE}" >> "${z_auth_keys}"
287	  chmod 600 "${z_auth_keys}"
288	
289	  buc_success "Key installed for ${BURH_ALIAS} in ${z_auth_keys}"
290	}
291	
292	######################################################################
293	# Furnish and Main
294	
295	zburh_furnish() {
296	  buc_doc_env "BURD_BUK_DIR          " "BUK module directory (dispatch-provided)"

298	  buc_doc_env_done || return 0

302	  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burc_regime.sh"
303	  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burs_regime.sh"

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# BURH CLI - Command line interface for BURH host regime operations
#
# Four command families:
#   burh_validate/render/list  — regime read operations (require BUZ_FOLIO)
#   burh_construct_*           — profile constructors (write burh.env)
#   burh_ssh_*/burh_verify_*   — SSH automation (config, verify)
#   burh_install_key           — authorized_keys management (per-profile, idempotent)

set -euo pipefail

source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buc_command.sh"

######################################################################
# Command Functions — Regime Operations

burh_validate() {
  buc_doc_brief "Validate BURH host regime profile via enrollment report"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || buc_die "Profile alias required (e.g., winhost-cyg)"
  buc_step "Validating BURH host regime"
  buv_report BURH "Host Regime"
  buc_step "BURH host regime valid"
}

burh_render() {
  buc_doc_brief "Display diagnostic view of BURH host regime profile"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || buc_die "Profile alias required (e.g., winhost-cyg)"
  buv_render BURH "BURH - Bash Utility Host Regime"
}

burh_list() {
  buc_doc_brief "List available BURH host profiles for current user"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  local z_aliases
  z_aliases=$(burh_list_capture) || buc_die "No BURH profiles found for user: ${BURS_USER}"
  buc_step "Available profiles for ${BURS_USER}:"
  local z_alias=""
  for z_alias in ${z_aliases}; do
    buc_bare "        ${z_alias}"
  done
}

######################################################################
# Internal: Constructor Helper
#
# Shared logic for all platform constructors.
# Args: host user moniker suffix command_value key_file
# command_value and key_file may be empty.

zburh_construct() {
  local z_host="${1:-}"
  local z_user="${2:-}"
  local z_moniker="${3:-}"
  local z_suffix="${4:-}"
  local z_command="${5:-}"
  local z_key_file="${6:-}"

  test -n "${z_host}"    || buc_die "host required (arg 1)"
  test -n "${z_user}"    || buc_die "user required (arg 2)"
  test -n "${z_moniker}" || buc_die "moniker required (arg 3)"

  local -r z_alias="${z_moniker}-${z_suffix}"
  local -r z_profile_dir="${BURD_CONFIG_DIR}/users/${BURS_USER}/${z_alias}"
  local -r z_profile_file="${z_profile_dir}/burh.env"
  local -r z_pubkey_file="${HOME}/.ssh/${z_alias}.pub"
  local z_pubkey=""
  local z_has_pubkey=0

  if test -f "${z_pubkey_file}"; then
    z_pubkey=$(head -1 "${z_pubkey_file}")
    z_has_pubkey=1
    buc_step "Found public key: ${z_pubkey_file}"
  else
    z_pubkey="ssh-ed25519 REPLACE_WITH_ACTUAL_PUBKEY ${BURS_USER}@$(hostname -s)"
  fi

  mkdir -p "${z_profile_dir}"

  printf '%s\n' "BURH_HOST=${z_host}"           >  "${z_profile_file}"
  printf '%s\n' "BURH_USER=${z_user}"            >> "${z_profile_file}"
  printf '%s\n' "BURH_ALIAS=${z_alias}"          >> "${z_profile_file}"
  printf '%s\n' "BURH_SSH_PUBKEY='${z_pubkey}'"  >> "${z_profile_file}"
  printf '%s\n' "BURH_KEY_FILE=${z_key_file}"    >> "${z_profile_file}"
  printf '%s\n' "BURH_COMMAND='${z_command}'"    >> "${z_profile_file}"

  buc_step "BURH profile written: ${z_profile_file}"

  if test "${z_has_pubkey}" = "0"; then
    buh_e
    buh_section "SSH Key Required"
    buyy_cmd_yawp "${z_pubkey_file}"; local -r z_pubkey_yelp="${z_buym_yelp}"
    buh_line "No public key at ${z_pubkey_yelp}."
    buh_line   "Generate one:"
    buh_e
    buh_code   "ssh-keygen -t ed25519 -f ~/.ssh/${z_alias}"
    buh_e
    buh_line   "Then re-run this constructor to populate the public key."
  fi
}

######################################################################
# Command Functions — Constructors

burh_construct_linux() {
  buc_doc_brief "Construct BURH profile for Linux target"
  buc_doc_param "host" "IP or hostname of the Linux machine"
  buc_doc_param "user" "Username on the remote host"
  buc_doc_param "moniker" "Short name (alias = moniker-linux)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  zburh_construct "${1:-}" "${2:-}" "${3:-}" "linux" ""
}

burh_construct_mac() {
  buc_doc_brief "Construct BURH profile for macOS target"
  buc_doc_param "host" "IP or hostname of the Mac"
  buc_doc_param "user" "Username on the remote host"
  buc_doc_param "moniker" "Short name (alias = moniker-mac)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  zburh_construct "${1:-}" "${2:-}" "${3:-}" "mac" ""
}

burh_construct_cygwin() {
  buc_doc_brief "Construct BURH profile for Windows Cygwin target"
  buc_doc_param "host" "IP or hostname of the Windows machine"
  buc_doc_param "user" "Username on the Windows host"
  buc_doc_param "moniker" "Short name (alias = moniker-cyg)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  zburh_construct "${1:-}" "${2:-}" "${3:-}" "cyg" 'C:\cygwin64\bin\bash.exe -l'
}

burh_construct_wsl() {
  buc_doc_brief "Construct BURH profile for Windows WSL target"
  buc_doc_param "host" "IP or hostname of the Windows machine"
  buc_doc_param "user" "Username on the Windows host"
  buc_doc_param "moniker" "Short name (alias = moniker-wsl)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  local -r z_wsl_distro="Ubuntu"
  zburh_construct "${1:-}" "${2:-}" "${3:-}" "wsl" "C:\\Windows\\System32\\wsl.exe -d ${z_wsl_distro}"
}

burh_construct_powershell() {
  buc_doc_brief "Construct BURH profile for Windows PowerShell target"
  buc_doc_param "host" "IP or hostname of the Windows machine"
  buc_doc_param "user" "Username on the Windows host"
  buc_doc_param "moniker" "Short name (alias = moniker-ps)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  zburh_construct "${1:-}" "${2:-}" "${3:-}" "ps" 'C:\Windows\System32\WindowsPowerShell\v1.0\powershell.exe'
}

burh_construct_localhost() {
  buc_doc_brief "Construct BURH profile for localhost (local SSH)"
  buc_doc_param "user" "Username on this machine"
  buc_doc_param "moniker" "Short name (alias = moniker-local)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  zburh_construct "localhost" "${1:-}" "${2:-}" "local" "" "id_ed25519"
}

######################################################################
# Command Functions — SSH Automation

burh_ssh_config() {
  buc_doc_brief "Generate SSH config entries from all BURH profiles"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  local z_aliases
  z_aliases=$(burh_list_capture) || buc_die "No BURH profiles found for user: ${BURS_USER}"

  local -r z_config="${HOME}/.ssh/config"
  local -r z_begin="# --- BEGIN BURH MANAGED ---"
  local -r z_end="# --- END BURH MANAGED ---"

  # Build managed section content
  local z_section=""
  z_section="${z_begin}"$'\n'
  z_section+="# Generated by buw-HWsc — do not edit this section manually"$'\n'

  local z_alias=""
  for z_alias in ${z_aliases}; do
    local z_profile="${BURD_CONFIG_DIR}/users/${BURS_USER}/${z_alias}/burh.env"
    # Source directly — BURH not kindled, no readonly conflict
    source "${z_profile}"
    z_section+=$'\n'
    local z_key="${BURH_KEY_FILE:-${BURH_ALIAS}}"
    z_section+="Host ${BURH_ALIAS}"$'\n'
    z_section+="  HostName ${BURH_HOST}"$'\n'
    z_section+="  User ${BURH_USER}"$'\n'
    z_section+="  IdentityFile ~/.ssh/${z_key}"$'\n'
  done

  z_section+="${z_end}"$'\n'

  # Write managed section into config
  mkdir -p "${HOME}/.ssh"

  if test -f "${z_config}" && grep -q "${z_begin}" "${z_config}"; then
    # Replace existing managed section
    local -r z_temp=$(mktemp)
    local z_in_managed=0
    while IFS= read -r z_line || test -n "${z_line}"; do
      if test "${z_line}" = "${z_begin}"; then
        z_in_managed=1
        printf '%s' "${z_section}" >> "${z_temp}"
        continue
      fi
      if test "${z_in_managed}" = "1"; then
        test "${z_line}" = "${z_end}" && z_in_managed=0
        continue
      fi
      printf '%s\n' "${z_line}" >> "${z_temp}"
    done < "${z_config}"
    mv "${z_temp}" "${z_config}"
  elif test -f "${z_config}"; then
    # Append managed section
    printf '\n%s' "${z_section}" >> "${z_config}"
  else
    # Create new config
    printf '%s' "${z_section}" > "${z_config}"
  fi

  chmod 600 "${z_config}"
  buc_step "SSH config updated: ${z_config}"
  buc_step "Managed profiles: ${z_aliases}"
}

burh_verify_ssh() {
  buc_doc_brief "Verify SSH connectivity to a BURH profile"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || buc_die "Profile alias required (e.g., winhost-cyg)"

  buc_step "Testing SSH to ${BURH_ALIAS} (${BURH_HOST})..."
  local z_result=""
  if z_result=$(ssh -o BatchMode=yes -o ConnectTimeout=10 "${BURH_ALIAS}" whoami 2>&1); then
    buc_success "SSH OK: ${BURH_ALIAS} -> ${z_result}"
  else
    buc_die "SSH FAILED: ${BURH_ALIAS} -> ${z_result}"
  fi
}

burh_install_key() {
  buc_doc_brief "Install BURH profile SSH key into authorized_keys (idempotent)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || buc_die "Profile alias required (e.g., winhost-cyg)"

  local -r z_auth_keys="${HOME}/.ssh/authorized_keys"

  zburh_build_key_line

  mkdir -p "${HOME}/.ssh"

  if test -f "${z_auth_keys}"; then
    local -r z_temp=$(mktemp)
    grep -v "${ZBURH_KEY_MARKER}" "${z_auth_keys}" > "${z_temp}" || true
    mv "${z_temp}" "${z_auth_keys}"
  fi

  printf '%s\n' "${ZBURH_KEY_LINE}" >> "${z_auth_keys}"
  chmod 600 "${z_auth_keys}"

  buc_success "Key installed for ${BURH_ALIAS} in ${z_auth_keys}"
}

######################################################################
# Furnish and Main

zburh_furnish() {
  buc_doc_env "BURD_BUK_DIR          " "BUK module directory (dispatch-provided)"
  buc_doc_env "BURD_CONFIG_DIR       " "BUK config directory (dispatch-provided)"
  buc_doc_env_done || return 0

  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buv_validation.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burd_regime.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burc_regime.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burs_regime.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burh_regime.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/bupr_PresentationRegime.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buym_yelp.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buh_handbook.sh"

  zbuv_kindle
  zburd_kindle
  zburd_enforce

  source "${BURD_REGIME_FILE}" || buc_die "Failed to source BURC: ${BURD_REGIME_FILE}"

  zburc_kindle
  zburc_enforce

  source "${BURD_STATION_FILE}" || buc_die "Failed to source BURS: ${BURD_STATION_FILE}"

  zburs_kindle
  zburs_enforce

  zbupr_kindle

  # If BUZ_FOLIO is set, load and kindle the specified profile
  if test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"; then
    local -r z_profile_file="${BURD_CONFIG_DIR}/users/${BURS_USER}/${BUZ_FOLIO}/burh.env"
    test -f "${z_profile_file}" || buc_die "BURH profile not found: ${z_profile_file}"
    source "${z_profile_file}" || buc_die "Failed to source BURH: ${z_profile_file}"
    zburh_kindle
    zburh_enforce
  fi
}

buc_execute burh_ "Bash Utility Host Regime" zburh_furnish "$@"

# eof

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# BUK Windows Commands - Command Line Interface
#
# Furnish: needs buhw tinder (Windows constants) and buc_require.
# No regime stack — commands consume constants directly.

set -euo pipefail

source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buc_command.sh"

zbuwc_furnish() {
  buc_doc_env "BURD_BUK_DIR          " "BUK module directory (dispatch-provided)"
  buc_doc_env "BURD_CONFIG_DIR       " "BUK config directory (dispatch-provided)"
  buc_doc_env_done || return 0

  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buv_validation.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burd_regime.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burc_regime.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burs_regime.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burh_regime.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buhw_windows.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buwc_windows.sh"

  zbuv_kindle
  zburd_kindle
  zburd_enforce

  source "${BURD_REGIME_FILE}" || buc_die "Failed to source BURC: ${BURD_REGIME_FILE}"

  zburc_kindle
  zburc_enforce

  source "${BURD_STATION_FILE}" || buc_die "Failed to source BURS: ${BURD_STATION_FILE}"

  zburs_kindle
  zburs_enforce

  zbuhw_kindle
  zbuwc_kindle

  # If BUZ_FOLIO is set, load and kindle the specified profile
  if test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"; then
    local -r z_profile_file="${BURD_CONFIG_DIR}/users/${BURS_USER}/${BUZ_FOLIO}/burh.env"
    test -f "${z_profile_file}" || buc_die "BURH profile not found: ${z_profile_file}"
    source "${z_profile_file}" || buc_die "Failed to source BURH: ${z_profile_file}"
    zburh_kindle
    zburh_enforce
  fi
}

buc_execute buwc_ "Windows Commands" zbuwc_furnish "$@"

# eof

139	    buc_step "Firewall rule already exists"

142	      || buc_die "Failed to create firewall rule"

145

147	  buc_step "Verifying loopback SSH..."
148	  local z_whoami=""

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# BUK Windows Commands
#
# Windows-specific automation commands that alter system configuration.
# These are commands (not handbooks) — they modify state on the target machine.
# Consumes buhw tinder constants for Windows paths and network constants.

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion detection
test -z "${ZBUWC_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module buwc multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
ZBUWC_SOURCED=1

######################################################################
# Internal: Kindle and Sentinel

zbuwc_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZBUWC_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module buwc already kindled"
  zbuhw_sentinel
  readonly ZBUWC_KINDLED=1
}

zbuwc_sentinel() {
  test "${ZBUWC_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module buwc not kindled - call zbuwc_kindle first"
}

######################################################################
# External Functions (buwc_*)

buwc_install_key() {
  zbuwc_sentinel

  buc_doc_brief "Install BURH profile SSH key into Windows administrators authorized_keys (idempotent)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || buc_die "Profile alias required (e.g., winhost-cyg)"

  command -v powershell.exe >/dev/null 2>&1 \
    || buc_die "powershell.exe not found — this command must run from WSL"

  zburh_build_key_line

  # Read existing file, remove old marker line, append new line — all via PowerShell
  local z_existing=""
  if z_existing=$(powershell.exe -Command "Get-Content -Path '${ZBUHW_ADMIN_AUTH_KEYS}' -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue" 2>/dev/null); then
    z_existing=$(printf '%s\n' "${z_existing}" | grep -v "${ZBURH_KEY_MARKER}" || true)
  fi

  {
    test -z "${z_existing}" || printf '%s\n' "${z_existing}"
    printf '%s\n' "${ZBURH_KEY_LINE}"
  } | powershell.exe -Command "\$input | Set-Content -Path '${ZBUHW_ADMIN_AUTH_KEYS}' -Force" \
    || buc_die "Failed to write authorized_keys (elevated terminal required)"

  # Windows admin authorized_keys requires specific ACL
  powershell.exe -Command "icacls '${ZBUHW_ADMIN_AUTH_KEYS}' /inheritance:r /grant 'Administrators:F' /grant 'SYSTEM:F'" \
    || buc_die "Failed to set authorized_keys permissions"

  buc_success "Key installed for ${BURH_ALIAS} in ${ZBUHW_ADMIN_AUTH_KEYS}"
}

buwc_bootstrap_sshd() {
  zbuwc_sentinel

  buc_doc_brief "Provision OpenSSH server on Windows via PowerShell from WSL"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  buc_require \
    "This will OVERWRITE sshd_config, restart sshd service, and create firewall rules on the Windows host." \
    "OVERWRITE"

  command -v powershell.exe >/dev/null 2>&1 \
    || buc_die "powershell.exe not found — this command must run from WSL"

  buc_step "Bootstrap: OpenSSH Server on Windows"

  # Step 1: Probe sshd
  buc_step "Probing OpenSSH Server..."
  local z_status=""
  if z_status=$(powershell.exe -Command 'Get-Service sshd -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Select-Object -ExpandProperty Status' 2>&1); then
    z_status=$(printf '%s' "${z_status}" | tr -d '\r\n')
    buc_step "OpenSSH Server status: ${z_status}"
  else
    buc_step "OpenSSH Server not installed — installing..."
    powershell.exe -Command 'Add-WindowsCapability -Online -Name OpenSSH.Server~~~~0.0.1.0' \
      || buc_die "Failed to install OpenSSH Server (elevated terminal required)"
    buc_step "OpenSSH Server installed"
  fi

  # Step 2: Write sshd_config from template
  buc_step "Writing sshd_config..."
  {
    echo "# Generated by buw-wcb — BURH bootstrap"
    echo "Port ${ZBUHW_SSH_PORT}"
    echo "PasswordAuthentication no"
    echo "PubkeyAuthentication yes"
    echo "PermitEmptyPasswords no"
    echo "AuthorizedKeysFile .ssh/authorized_keys"
    echo "Subsystem sftp sftp-server.exe"
    echo "Match Group administrators"
    echo "  AuthorizedKeysFile __PROGRAMDATA__/ssh/administrators_authorized_keys"
  } | powershell.exe -Command "\$input | Set-Content -Path '${ZBUHW_SSHD_CONFIG}' -Force" \
    || buc_die "Failed to write sshd_config (elevated terminal required)"
  buc_step "sshd_config written"

  # Validate sshd_config
  powershell.exe -Command "sshd -t -f '${ZBUHW_SSHD_CONFIG}'" \
    || buc_die "sshd_config validation failed — check config syntax"
  buc_step "sshd_config validated"

  # Step 3: Configure and start sshd service
  buc_step "Configuring sshd service..."
  powershell.exe -Command 'Set-Service -Name sshd -StartupType Automatic' \
    || buc_die "Failed to configure sshd service"
  powershell.exe -Command 'Restart-Service sshd' \
    || buc_die "Failed to restart sshd service"
  buc_step "sshd service configured and started"

  # Step 4: Firewall rule (idempotent)
  buc_step "Checking firewall rule..."
  if powershell.exe -Command "Get-NetFirewallRule -Name '${ZBUHW_FW_RULE_NAME}' -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    buc_step "Firewall rule already exists"
  else
    powershell.exe -Command "New-NetFirewallRule -Name '${ZBUHW_FW_RULE_NAME}' -DisplayName '${ZBUHW_FW_DISPLAY_NAME}' -Enabled True -Direction Inbound -Protocol TCP -Action Allow -LocalPort ${ZBUHW_SSH_PORT}" \
      || buc_die "Failed to create firewall rule"
    buc_step "Firewall rule created"
  fi

  # Step 5: Loopback verify
  buc_step "Verifying loopback SSH..."
  local z_whoami=""
  if z_whoami=$(ssh -o BatchMode=yes -o ConnectTimeout=5 -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no localhost whoami 2>&1); then
    z_whoami=$(printf '%s' "${z_whoami}" | tr -d '\r\n')
    buc_success "Bootstrap complete — loopback SSH: ${z_whoami}"
  else
    buc_warn "Loopback verify deferred (keys may need setup from client machine)"
    buc_step "Bootstrap structurally complete"
  fi
}

# eof
