1	#!/bin/bash
2	#
3	# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
4	#
5	# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
6	# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
7	# You may obtain a copy of the License at
8	#
9	#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
10	#
11	# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
12	# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
13	# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
14	# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
15	# limitations under the License.
16	#
17	# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
18	#
19	# Bash Utility Handbook - Windows OS Procedures
20	#
21	# Generic Windows mechanisms for SSH access, WSL, and Cygwin setup.
22	# All output is handbook display (buh_* combinators only).
23	# Project-specific topology and Docker policy live in rbhw_windows.sh.
24	
25	set -euo pipefail
26	
27	# Multiple inclusion detection
28	test -z "${ZBUHW_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module buhw multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
29	ZBUHW_SOURCED=1
30	
31	######################################################################
32	# Internal: Kindle and Sentinel
33	
34	zbuhw_kindle() {
35	  test -z "${ZBUHW_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module buhw already kindled"
36	
37	  # Tinder — fixed Windows paths and network constants
38	  # Forward slashes throughout — works in PowerShell, display, and icacls
39	  readonly ZBUHW_SSHD_CONFIG='C:/ProgramData/ssh/sshd_config'
40	  readonly ZBUHW_ADMIN_AUTH_KEYS='C:/ProgramData/ssh/administrators_authorized_keys'
41	  readonly ZBUHW_CYGWIN_ROOT='C:/cygwin64'
42	  readonly ZBUHW_CYGWIN_BASH='C:/cygwin64/bin/bash.exe'
43	  readonly ZBUHW_SSH_PORT="22"
44	  readonly ZBUHW_FW_RULE_NAME="sshd"
45	  readonly ZBUHW_FW_DISPLAY_NAME="OpenSSH Server"
46	
47	  readonly ZBUHW_KINDLED=1
48	}
49	
50	zbuhw_sentinel() {
51	  test "${ZBUHW_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module buhw not kindled - call zbuhw_kindle first"
52	}
53	
54	######################################################################
55	# External Functions (buhw_*)
56	
57	buhw_access_base() {
58	  zbuhw_sentinel
59	
60	  buc_doc_brief "Display OpenSSH server installation and lockdown procedure"
61	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
62	
63	  buh_section  "OpenSSH Server Installation & Lockdown"
64	  buh_line     "Establish Windows as a keys-only SSH endpoint."
65	  buh_e
66	  buh_section  "Preconditions:"
67	  buh_line     "- Windows host with administrator access"
68	  buh_line     "- Network reachable on TCP/${ZBUHW_SSH_PORT}"
69	  buh_e
70	  buh_step1    "Open Elevated PowerShell:"
71	  buh_line     "Right-click Start → Terminal (Admin), or search 'PowerShell' and Run as Administrator."
72	  buh_line     "All commands below run in this elevated session."
73	  buh_e
74	  buh_step1    "Install OpenSSH Server:"
75	  buh_line     "This downloads from Windows Update — may take 10+ minutes."
76	  buh_code     "Add-WindowsCapability -Online -Name OpenSSH.Server~~~~0.0.1.0"
77	  buh_e
78	  buh_step1    "Enable OpenSSH Server:"
79	  buh_code     "Start-Service sshd"
80	  buh_code     "Set-Service -Name sshd -StartupType Automatic"
81	  buh_e
82	  buh_step1    "Allow Port ${ZBUHW_SSH_PORT} Through Firewall:"
83	  buh_code     "New-NetFirewallRule -Name ${ZBUHW_FW_RULE_NAME} -DisplayName \"${ZBUHW_FW_DISPLAY_NAME}\" -Enabled True -Direction Inbound -Protocol TCP -Action Allow -LocalPort ${ZBUHW_SSH_PORT}"
84	  buh_e
85	  buh_step1    "Configure sshd_config:"
86	  buyy_cmd_yawp "${ZBUHW_SSHD_CONFIG}"; local -r z_sshd_config_yelp="${z_buym_yelp}"
87	  buh_line     "File: ${z_sshd_config_yelp}"
88	  buh_line     "This file is SYSTEM-owned. Edit a temp copy, then replace."
89	  buh_e
90	  buh_step2    "Copy to editable location:"
91	  buh_code     "Copy-Item ${ZBUHW_SSHD_CONFIG} \$env:TEMP\\sshd_config"
92	  buh_e
93	  buh_step2    "Edit the copy:"
94	  buh_code     "notepad \$env:TEMP\\sshd_config"
95	  buh_line     "Set these directives (add if not present):"
96	  buh_code     "PasswordAuthentication no"
97	  buh_code     "PubkeyAuthentication yes"
98	  buh_code     "PermitEmptyPasswords no"
99	  buh_line     "Do NOT add UsePAM or ChallengeResponseAuthentication — Windows OpenSSH"
100	  buh_line     "rejects unrecognized directives and the service will fail to start."
101	  buh_e
102	  buh_step2    "Validate before applying:"
103	  buh_code     "sshd -t -f \$env:TEMP\\sshd_config"
104	  buh_line     "Expect: no output (silence means valid). Fix any reported errors."
105	  buh_e
106	  buh_step2    "Replace the original:"
107	  buh_code     "Copy-Item \$env:TEMP\\sshd_config ${ZBUHW_SSHD_CONFIG} -Force"
108	  buh_e
109	  buh_step1    "Restart Service:"
110	  buh_code     "Restart-Service sshd"
111	  buh_e
112	  buh_step1    "Verify:"
113	  buh_line     "Discover your Windows username and IP (on the Windows machine):"
114	  buh_code     "whoami"
115	  buh_code     "ipconfig"
116	  buh_line     "Note the username (after the backslash) and IPv4 address."
117	  buh_e
118	  buh_line     "From a remote machine, test SSH reachability:"
119	  buh_code     "ssh <username>@<ip>"
120	  buh_line     "Expect: Permission denied (publickey). This confirms sshd is running"
121	  buh_line     "and password login is correctly rejected. Key setup follows in step 2."
122	
123	}
124	
125	buhw_access_remote() {
126	  zbuhw_sentinel
127	
128	  buc_doc_brief "Display SSH client key generation and host config procedure (args: host user key-name alias)"
129	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
130	
131	  local z_host="${1:-}"
132	  local z_user="${2:-}"
133	  local z_key_name="${3:-}"
134	  local z_alias="${4:-}"
135	
136	  test -n "${z_host}"     || buc_die "buhw_access_remote: host required (arg 1)"
137	  test -n "${z_user}"     || buc_die "buhw_access_remote: user required (arg 2)"
138	  test -n "${z_key_name}" || buc_die "buhw_access_remote: key-name required (arg 3)"
139	  test -n "${z_alias}"    || buc_die "buhw_access_remote: alias required (arg 4)"
140	
141	  buh_section  "SSH Client Key & Host Configuration"
142	  buh_line     "Provision client with a dedicated key and deterministic host config."
143	  buh_e
144	  buh_section  "Parameters:"
145	  buyy_cmd_yawp "${z_host}"; local -r z_host_yelp="${z_buym_yelp}"
146	  buh_line     "  Host:     ${z_host_yelp}"
147	  buyy_cmd_yawp "${z_user}"; local -r z_user_yelp="${z_buym_yelp}"
148	  buh_line     "  User:     ${z_user_yelp}"
149	  buyy_cmd_yawp "${z_key_name}"; local -r z_key_name_yelp="${z_buym_yelp}"
150	  buh_line     "  Key name: ${z_key_name_yelp}"
151	  buyy_cmd_yawp "${z_alias}"; local -r z_alias_yelp="${z_buym_yelp}"
152	  buh_line     "  Alias:    ${z_alias_yelp}"
153	  buh_e
154	  buh_step1    "Generate Key:"
155	  buh_code     "ssh-keygen -t ed25519 -f ~/.ssh/${z_key_name}"
156	  buh_e
157	  buh_step1    "Create SSH Config Entry:"
158	  buyy_cmd_yawp "~/.ssh/config"; local -r z_ssh_config_yelp="${z_buym_yelp}"
159	  buh_line     "Add to ${z_ssh_config_yelp}"
160	  buh_e
161	  buh_code     "Host ${z_alias}"
162	  buh_code     "  HostName ${z_host}"
163	  buh_code     "  User ${z_user}"
164	  buh_code     "  IdentityFile ~/.ssh/${z_key_name}"
165	  buh_e
166	  buh_step1    "Copy Public Key to Host:"
167	  buh_line     "The public key must be added to the host's authorized_keys."
168	  buyy_cmd_yawp "cat ~/.ssh/${z_key_name}.pub"; local -r z_pubkey_cmd_yelp="${z_buym_yelp}"
169	  buh_line     "Display the key: ${z_pubkey_cmd_yelp}"
170	  buh_line     "Copy the output for use in the entrypoints procedure."
171	  buh_e
172	  buh_section  "Verification:"
173	  buh_code     "ssh ${z_alias}"
174	  buh_line     "Expect: connects (may not yet enter target env — routing not configured)."
175	
176	}

196	  buh_e

198	  buh_code     "command=\"wsl.exe -d DISTRO\"        ssh-ed25519 BBBB... wsl"

202	  buh_line     "Replace ${z_distro_placeholder_yelp} with your WSL distribution name."
203	  buyy_ui_yawp "AAAA.../BBBB.../CCCC..."; local -r z_key_placeholders_yelp="${z_buym_yelp}"
204	  buh_line     "Replace ${z_key_placeholders_yelp} with actual public key content."
205	  buh_e
206	  buh_step1    "Set File Permissions:"
207	  buh_line     "Run in an elevated PowerShell:"
208	  buh_code     "icacls \"${ZBUHW_ADMIN_AUTH_KEYS}\" /inheritance:r"
209	  buh_code     "icacls \"${ZBUHW_ADMIN_AUTH_KEYS}\" /grant \"Administrators:F\""

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# Bash Utility Handbook - Windows OS Procedures
#
# Generic Windows mechanisms for SSH access, WSL, and Cygwin setup.
# All output is handbook display (buh_* combinators only).
# Project-specific topology and Docker policy live in rbhw_windows.sh.

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion detection
test -z "${ZBUHW_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module buhw multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
ZBUHW_SOURCED=1

######################################################################
# Internal: Kindle and Sentinel

zbuhw_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZBUHW_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module buhw already kindled"

  # Tinder — fixed Windows paths and network constants
  # Forward slashes throughout — works in PowerShell, display, and icacls
  readonly ZBUHW_SSHD_CONFIG='C:/ProgramData/ssh/sshd_config'
  readonly ZBUHW_ADMIN_AUTH_KEYS='C:/ProgramData/ssh/administrators_authorized_keys'
  readonly ZBUHW_CYGWIN_ROOT='C:/cygwin64'
  readonly ZBUHW_CYGWIN_BASH='C:/cygwin64/bin/bash.exe'
  readonly ZBUHW_SSH_PORT="22"
  readonly ZBUHW_FW_RULE_NAME="sshd"
  readonly ZBUHW_FW_DISPLAY_NAME="OpenSSH Server"

  readonly ZBUHW_KINDLED=1
}

zbuhw_sentinel() {
  test "${ZBUHW_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module buhw not kindled - call zbuhw_kindle first"
}

######################################################################
# External Functions (buhw_*)

buhw_access_base() {
  zbuhw_sentinel

  buc_doc_brief "Display OpenSSH server installation and lockdown procedure"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  buh_section  "OpenSSH Server Installation & Lockdown"
  buh_line     "Establish Windows as a keys-only SSH endpoint."
  buh_e
  buh_section  "Preconditions:"
  buh_line     "- Windows host with administrator access"
  buh_line     "- Network reachable on TCP/${ZBUHW_SSH_PORT}"
  buh_e
  buh_step1    "Open Elevated PowerShell:"
  buh_line     "Right-click Start → Terminal (Admin), or search 'PowerShell' and Run as Administrator."
  buh_line     "All commands below run in this elevated session."
  buh_e
  buh_step1    "Install OpenSSH Server:"
  buh_line     "This downloads from Windows Update — may take 10+ minutes."
  buh_code     "Add-WindowsCapability -Online -Name OpenSSH.Server~~~~0.0.1.0"
  buh_e
  buh_step1    "Enable OpenSSH Server:"
  buh_code     "Start-Service sshd"
  buh_code     "Set-Service -Name sshd -StartupType Automatic"
  buh_e
  buh_step1    "Allow Port ${ZBUHW_SSH_PORT} Through Firewall:"
  buh_code     "New-NetFirewallRule -Name ${ZBUHW_FW_RULE_NAME} -DisplayName \"${ZBUHW_FW_DISPLAY_NAME}\" -Enabled True -Direction Inbound -Protocol TCP -Action Allow -LocalPort ${ZBUHW_SSH_PORT}"
  buh_e
  buh_step1    "Configure sshd_config:"
  buyy_cmd_yawp "${ZBUHW_SSHD_CONFIG}"; local -r z_sshd_config_yelp="${z_buym_yelp}"
  buh_line     "File: ${z_sshd_config_yelp}"
  buh_line     "This file is SYSTEM-owned. Edit a temp copy, then replace."
  buh_e
  buh_step2    "Copy to editable location:"
  buh_code     "Copy-Item ${ZBUHW_SSHD_CONFIG} \$env:TEMP\\sshd_config"
  buh_e
  buh_step2    "Edit the copy:"
  buh_code     "notepad \$env:TEMP\\sshd_config"
  buh_line     "Set these directives (add if not present):"
  buh_code     "PasswordAuthentication no"
  buh_code     "PubkeyAuthentication yes"
  buh_code     "PermitEmptyPasswords no"
  buh_line     "Do NOT add UsePAM or ChallengeResponseAuthentication — Windows OpenSSH"
  buh_line     "rejects unrecognized directives and the service will fail to start."
  buh_e
  buh_step2    "Validate before applying:"
  buh_code     "sshd -t -f \$env:TEMP\\sshd_config"
  buh_line     "Expect: no output (silence means valid). Fix any reported errors."
  buh_e
  buh_step2    "Replace the original:"
  buh_code     "Copy-Item \$env:TEMP\\sshd_config ${ZBUHW_SSHD_CONFIG} -Force"
  buh_e
  buh_step1    "Restart Service:"
  buh_code     "Restart-Service sshd"
  buh_e
  buh_step1    "Verify:"
  buh_line     "Discover your Windows username and IP (on the Windows machine):"
  buh_code     "whoami"
  buh_code     "ipconfig"
  buh_line     "Note the username (after the backslash) and IPv4 address."
  buh_e
  buh_line     "From a remote machine, test SSH reachability:"
  buh_code     "ssh <username>@<ip>"
  buh_line     "Expect: Permission denied (publickey). This confirms sshd is running"
  buh_line     "and password login is correctly rejected. Key setup follows in step 2."

}

buhw_access_remote() {
  zbuhw_sentinel

  buc_doc_brief "Display SSH client key generation and host config procedure (args: host user key-name alias)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  local z_host="${1:-}"
  local z_user="${2:-}"
  local z_key_name="${3:-}"
  local z_alias="${4:-}"

  test -n "${z_host}"     || buc_die "buhw_access_remote: host required (arg 1)"
  test -n "${z_user}"     || buc_die "buhw_access_remote: user required (arg 2)"
  test -n "${z_key_name}" || buc_die "buhw_access_remote: key-name required (arg 3)"
  test -n "${z_alias}"    || buc_die "buhw_access_remote: alias required (arg 4)"

  buh_section  "SSH Client Key & Host Configuration"
  buh_line     "Provision client with a dedicated key and deterministic host config."
  buh_e
  buh_section  "Parameters:"
  buyy_cmd_yawp "${z_host}"; local -r z_host_yelp="${z_buym_yelp}"
  buh_line     "  Host:     ${z_host_yelp}"
  buyy_cmd_yawp "${z_user}"; local -r z_user_yelp="${z_buym_yelp}"
  buh_line     "  User:     ${z_user_yelp}"
  buyy_cmd_yawp "${z_key_name}"; local -r z_key_name_yelp="${z_buym_yelp}"
  buh_line     "  Key name: ${z_key_name_yelp}"
  buyy_cmd_yawp "${z_alias}"; local -r z_alias_yelp="${z_buym_yelp}"
  buh_line     "  Alias:    ${z_alias_yelp}"
  buh_e
  buh_step1    "Generate Key:"
  buh_code     "ssh-keygen -t ed25519 -f ~/.ssh/${z_key_name}"
  buh_e
  buh_step1    "Create SSH Config Entry:"
  buyy_cmd_yawp "~/.ssh/config"; local -r z_ssh_config_yelp="${z_buym_yelp}"
  buh_line     "Add to ${z_ssh_config_yelp}"
  buh_e
  buh_code     "Host ${z_alias}"
  buh_code     "  HostName ${z_host}"
  buh_code     "  User ${z_user}"
  buh_code     "  IdentityFile ~/.ssh/${z_key_name}"
  buh_e
  buh_step1    "Copy Public Key to Host:"
  buh_line     "The public key must be added to the host's authorized_keys."
  buyy_cmd_yawp "cat ~/.ssh/${z_key_name}.pub"; local -r z_pubkey_cmd_yelp="${z_buym_yelp}"
  buh_line     "Display the key: ${z_pubkey_cmd_yelp}"
  buh_line     "Copy the output for use in the entrypoints procedure."
  buh_e
  buh_section  "Verification:"
  buh_code     "ssh ${z_alias}"
  buh_line     "Expect: connects (may not yet enter target env — routing not configured)."

}

buhw_access_entrypoints() {
  zbuhw_sentinel

  buc_doc_brief "Display SSH command= routing format and key permissions procedure"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  buh_section  "SSH Entrypoint Routing via command= Prefix"
  buh_line     "Deterministically route SSH keys to specific environments."
  buh_line     "Each key forces a single environment — no interactive shell selection."
  buh_e
  buh_section  "Preconditions:"
  buh_line     "- OpenSSH server installed (access-base complete)"
  buh_line     "- Public keys available from access-remote"
  buh_e
  buh_step1    "Edit Administrators Authorized Keys:"
  buyy_cmd_yawp "${ZBUHW_ADMIN_AUTH_KEYS}"; local -r z_admin_auth_keys_yelp="${z_buym_yelp}"
  buh_line     "File: ${z_admin_auth_keys_yelp}"
  buh_line     "Add one line per environment, prefixed with command= directive:"
  buh_e
  buh_code     "command=\"${ZBUHW_CYGWIN_BASH} -l\" ssh-ed25519 AAAA... cygwin"
  buh_code     "command=\"wsl.exe -d DISTRO\"        ssh-ed25519 BBBB... wsl"
  buh_code     "command=\"powershell.exe\"            ssh-ed25519 CCCC... windows"
  buh_e
  buyy_ui_yawp "DISTRO"; local -r z_distro_placeholder_yelp="${z_buym_yelp}"
  buh_line     "Replace ${z_distro_placeholder_yelp} with your WSL distribution name."
  buyy_ui_yawp "AAAA.../BBBB.../CCCC..."; local -r z_key_placeholders_yelp="${z_buym_yelp}"
  buh_line     "Replace ${z_key_placeholders_yelp} with actual public key content."
  buh_e
  buh_step1    "Set File Permissions:"
  buh_line     "Run in an elevated PowerShell:"
  buh_code     "icacls \"${ZBUHW_ADMIN_AUTH_KEYS}\" /inheritance:r"
  buh_code     "icacls \"${ZBUHW_ADMIN_AUTH_KEYS}\" /grant \"Administrators:F\""
  buh_e
  buh_section  "Verification:"
  buh_line     "Test each key lands in the correct environment:"
  buh_code     "ssh -i key-cygwin host   # lands in Cygwin bash"
  buh_code     "ssh -i key-wsl host      # lands in WSL"
  buh_code     "ssh -i key-win host      # lands in PowerShell"

}

buhw_environment_wsl() {
  zbuhw_sentinel

  buc_doc_brief "Display WSL distribution creation procedure (arg: distro-name)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  local z_distro="${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"
  test -n "${z_distro}" || buc_die "buhw_environment_wsl: distro-name required"

  buh_section  "WSL Distribution Setup"
  buyy_cmd_yawp "${z_distro}"; local -r z_distro_create_yelp="${z_buym_yelp}"
  buh_line     "Create canonical Linux environment: ${z_distro_create_yelp}"
  buh_e
  buh_step1    "Enable WSL (if not already):"
  buh_line     "Run in an elevated PowerShell:"
  buh_code     "wsl --install"
  buh_e
  buh_step1    "Install Base Ubuntu:"
  buh_code     "wsl --install -d Ubuntu"
  buh_e
  buh_step1    "Export and Re-import as Named Distribution:"
  buh_code     "wsl --export Ubuntu ubuntu.tar"
  buh_code     "wsl --import ${z_distro} C:\\\\WSL\\\\${z_distro} ubuntu.tar"
  buh_e
  buh_step1    "Enable systemd:"
  buyy_cmd_yawp "${z_distro}"; local -r z_distro_inside_yelp="${z_buym_yelp}"
  buh_line     "Inside the distro (wsl -d ${z_distro_inside_yelp}):"
  buh_code     "sudo nano /etc/wsl.conf"
  buh_line     "Add:"
  buh_code     "[boot]"
  buh_code     "systemd=true"
  buh_e
  buh_step1    "Restart WSL:"
  buh_code     "wsl --shutdown"
  buh_e
  buh_section  "Verification:"
  buh_code     "wsl -d ${z_distro} systemctl is-system-running"
  buh_line     "Expect: running or degraded (acceptable)."

}

buhw_environment_cygwin() {
  zbuhw_sentinel

  buc_doc_brief "Display Cygwin POSIX userland installation procedure"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  buh_section  "Cygwin Installation"
  buh_line     "Install POSIX userland for orchestration testing."
  buh_e
  buh_step1    "Download and Run Cygwin Installer:"
  buh_link     "" "Cygwin Setup (64-bit)" "https://cygwin.com/setup-x86_64.exe"
  buyy_cmd_yawp "${ZBUHW_CYGWIN_ROOT}"; local -r z_cygwin_root_yelp="${z_buym_yelp}"
  buh_line     "Install to: ${z_cygwin_root_yelp}"
  buh_e
  buh_step1    "Required Packages:"
  buh_line     "Select these during installation:"
  buh_code     "bash"
  buh_code     "openssl"
  buh_code     "curl"
  buh_e
  buh_step1    "Verify Installation:"
  buh_line     "Launch Cygwin bash:"
  buh_code     "${ZBUHW_CYGWIN_BASH} -l"
  buh_e
  buh_section  "Verification:"
  buh_code     "openssl version"
  buh_code     "bash --version"
  buh_line     "Expect: bash version >= 3.2"

}

buhw_handbook_top() {
  zbuhw_sentinel

  buc_doc_brief "Display BUK top-level handbook index"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  buh_section  "Bash Utility Kit Handbook"
  buh_line     "OS-level procedures across all platforms."
  buh_e
  buh_index_buk

}

buhw_top() {
  zbuhw_sentinel

  buc_doc_brief "Display BUK-level Windows OS procedures checklist"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  buh_section  "Windows OS Procedures (BUK)"
  buh_line     "Generic Windows mechanisms — SSH access, WSL, and Cygwin."
  buh_line     "These procedures are OS-level and project-independent."
  buh_e
  buh_section  "BURH Profile Constructors:"
  buh_tt       "  Linux:      " "${BUWZ_RHC_LINUX}" "" " <host> <user> <moniker>"
  buh_tt       "  macOS:      " "${BUWZ_RHC_MAC}" "" " <host> <user> <moniker>"
  buh_tt       "  Cygwin:     " "${BUWZ_RHC_CYGWIN}" "" " <host> <user> <moniker>"
  buh_tt       "  WSL:        " "${BUWZ_RHC_WSL}" "" " <host> <user> <moniker>"
  buh_tt       "  PowerShell: " "${BUWZ_RHC_POWERSHELL}" "" " <host> <user> <moniker>"
  buh_tt       "  Localhost:  " "${BUWZ_RHC_LOCALHOST}" "" " <user> <moniker>"
  buh_e
  buh_section  "SSH Automation:"
  buh_tt       "  Write SSH config:       " "${BUWZ_HW_SSH_CONFIG}"
  buh_tt       "  Verify SSH connection:  " "${BUWZ_HW_VERIFY_SSH}" "" " <alias>"
  buh_tt       "  Install BURH key:       " "${BUWZ_RH_INSTALL_KEY}" "" " <alias>"
  buh_e
  buh_section  "Windows Commands:"
  buh_tt       "  Bootstrap sshd (WSL):   " "${BUWZ_WC_BOOTSTRAP}"
  buh_e
  buh_section  "Handbook Procedures (manual steps):"
  buh_tt       "  OpenSSH server install:                " "${BUWZ_HW_ACCESS_BASE}"
  buh_tt       "  SSH client key & host config:          " "${BUWZ_HW_ACCESS_REMOTE}"
  buh_tt       "  SSH entrypoint routing (command=):     " "${BUWZ_HW_ACCESS_ENTRY}"
  buh_e
  buh_section  "Environment Procedures:"
  buh_tt       "  WSL distribution setup:                " "${BUWZ_HW_ENV_WSL}"
  buh_tt       "  Cygwin installation:                   " "${BUWZ_HW_ENV_CYGWIN}"

}

# eof

#
# BUK Windows Handbook - Command Line Interface
#
# Thin furnish: handbook display procedures need only buh_* combinators
# and the buhw module tinder constants. No regime, no validation stack.

set -euo pipefail

source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buc_command.sh"

zbuhw_furnish() {
  buc_doc_env "BURD_BUK_DIR          " "BUK module directory (dispatch-provided)"
  buc_doc_env_done || return 0

  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buym_yelp.sh"          || buc_die "Failed to source buym_yelp.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buh_handbook.sh"       || buc_die "Failed to source buh_handbook.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buhw_windows.sh"       || buc_die "Failed to source buhw_windows.sh"

  zbuhw_kindle
}

buc_execute buhw_ "Windows Handbook" zbuhw_furnish "$@"

# eof

140	# Public: Yelp-aware tabtarget display
141	#
142	# buh_tt prefix colophon [imprint] [args]
143	#   Resolves tabtarget via yawp, displays with prefix.
144	
145	buh_tt() {
146	  zbuh_sentinel
147	  buyy_tt_yawp "${2:-}" "${3:-}" "${4:-}"
148	  local -r z_tt="${z_buym_yelp}"
149	  buh_line "${1:-}${z_tt}"
150	}
151	
152	######################################################################
153	# Public: Index renderers
154	#
155	# Group-level handbook indexes shared across kits.
156	# Each function renders one kit's handbook group tops.
157	
158	buh_index_buk() {
159	  buh_section  "Generic OS Procedures (Bash Utility Kit)"
160	  buh_line     "  Project-independent OS-level mechanisms."
161	  buh_tt       "  Windows:  " "${BUWZ_HW_TOP}"
162	}
163	
164	######################################################################
165	# Public: Pre-composed line output
166	#
167	# buh_line string
168	#   Indent-aware (respects z_buh_body_indent from step context).
169	#   Designed for lines pre-composed from yelp capture fragments
170	#   via direct bash variable interpolation:
171	#     buh_line "A ${RBYC_DEPOT} is where images live."
172	#   Yelps are pre-rendered (literal ESC bytes) and must not contain %.
173	
174	buh_line() {
175	  zbuh_sentinel
176	  buyf_format_yawp "${BUYC_RESET}" "${1:-}"
177	  printf '%s\n' "${z_buh_body_indent}${z_buym_format}" >&2
178	}
179

# Public: Yelp-aware tabtarget display
#
# buh_tt prefix colophon [imprint] [args]
#   Resolves tabtarget via yawp, displays with prefix.

buh_tt() {
  zbuh_sentinel
  buyy_tt_yawp "${2:-}" "${3:-}" "${4:-}"
  local -r z_tt="${z_buym_yelp}"
  buh_line "${1:-}${z_tt}"
}

######################################################################
# Public: Index renderers
#
# Group-level handbook indexes shared across kits.
# Each function renders one kit's handbook group tops.

buh_index_buk() {
  buh_section  "Generic OS Procedures (Bash Utility Kit)"
  buh_line     "  Project-independent OS-level mechanisms."
  buh_tt       "  Windows:  " "${BUWZ_HW_TOP}"
}

######################################################################
# Public: Pre-composed line output
#
# buh_line string
#   Indent-aware (respects z_buh_body_indent from step context).
#   Designed for lines pre-composed from yelp capture fragments
#   via direct bash variable interpolation:
#     buh_line "A ${RBYC_DEPOT} is where images live."
#   Yelps are pre-rendered (literal ESC bytes) and must not contain %.

buh_line() {
  zbuh_sentinel
  buyf_format_yawp "${BUYC_RESET}" "${1:-}"
  printf '%s\n' "${z_buh_body_indent}${z_buym_format}" >&2
}

# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.

#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# Recipe Bottle Windows Handbook - rbhw_top function

set -euo pipefail

test -z "${ZRBHW0_SOURCED:-}" || return 0
ZRBHW0_SOURCED=1

rbhw_top() {
  zrbhw_sentinel

  buc_doc_brief "Display Windows handbook orchestrator — full setup sequence"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  buh_section  "Windows Test Infrastructure Setup"
  buh_line     "Complete setup sequence for running Recipe Bottle tests on a Windows host."
  buyy_cmd_yawp "${ZRBHW_WSL_DISTRO}"; local -r z_wsl_distro_yelp="${z_buym_yelp}"
  buh_line "   Target WSL distro: ${z_wsl_distro_yelp}"
  buyy_cmd_yawp "${ZRBHW_DOCKER_CONTEXT}"; local -r z_docker_context_yelp="${z_buym_yelp}"
  buh_line "   Docker context:    ${z_docker_context_yelp}"
  buh_e
  buh_section  "Phase 1: SSH Access (BUK — generic OS)"
  buh_tt       "  1. OpenSSH server install & lockdown:  " "${BUWZ_HW_ACCESS_BASE}"
  buh_tt       "  2. SSH client key & host config:       " "${BUWZ_HW_ACCESS_REMOTE}"
  buh_tt       "  3. SSH entrypoint routing (command=):  " "${BUWZ_HW_ACCESS_ENTRY}"
  buh_e
  buh_section  "Phase 2: Environments (BUK — generic OS)"
  buh_tt       "  4. WSL distribution setup:             " "${BUWZ_HW_ENV_WSL}"
  buh_line "     Pass distro name: ${z_wsl_distro_yelp}"
  buh_tt       "  5. Cygwin installation:                " "${BUWZ_HW_ENV_CYGWIN}"
  buh_e
  buh_section  "Phase 3: User Provisioning (JJK — fundus accounts)"
  buh_line     "  6. Fundus user provisioning runs inside WSL:"
  buh_tt       "     Phase 1 (create users):             " "${JJZ_FUNDUS_PHASE1}"
  buh_tt       "     Phase 2 (clone repos):              " "${JJZ_FUNDUS_PHASE2}"
  buh_e
  buh_section  "Phase 4: Docker (RBK — project-specific)"
  buh_tt       "  7. Docker Desktop install:             " "${RBZ_HW_DOCKER_DESKTOP}"
  buh_tt       "  8. Native dockerd in WSL:              " "${RBZ_HW_DOCKER_WSL_NATIVE}"
  buh_line "     Pass distro name: ${z_wsl_distro_yelp}"
  buh_tt       "  9. Docker context discipline:          " "${RBZ_HW_DOCKER_CONTEXT}"

}

content.text:
Disk survey (threshold 85%): 228 GB: 80%
Heat: rbk-mvp-3-draft-windows-procedures (₣A-)
Status: racing
Created: 260412


## Paces

### practice-access-procedures (₢A-AAC) [rough]

## Character
Human-driven with agent amending — running automation tabtargets on real hardware, discovering gaps.

## Docket
Run the new SSH automation tabtargets against the actual Windows host.

**Mac side:**
1. Run platform constructors per BURH profile — creates `burh.env`, displays `ssh-keygen` commands if pubkey missing
2. User runs `ssh-keygen` manually (copy/paste from constructor output)
3. Re-run constructors — picks up `.pub` files, writes complete `burh.env`
4. `buw-HWsc` — write `~/.ssh/config` entries from all profiles
5. Commit + push (BURH profiles now carry real pubkeys)

**Windows/WSL side:**
Git pull in WSL, then run `buw-HWbs`.

Note: sshd is already configured from manual AccessBase practice (steps 1-7). Running `buw-HWbs` will overwrite `sshd_config` and `authorized_keys` with BURH-derived versions. This validates that automation produces a working result, but replaces manual config. Human decides.

**Verify:**
- `buw-HWvs` per profile — ssh via each alias, confirm correct environment lands
- `jjx_bind` to Windows host — confirm relay works

Agent amends tabtarget code for any issues discovered during practice. This is the first real cross-boundary execution — expect discoveries.

### specify-bus0-buwc-module-and-key-builder (₢A-AAR) [rough]

## Character
Spec writing — adding infrastructure-level definitions to BUS0. Mechanical once patterns are understood from the study phase in ₢A-AAQ.

## Docket

Add two infrastructure-level specifications to BUS0:

**1. BUWC Module Declaration**
Define the Windows Commands module as a category in BUS0, parallel to how BURH Regime is declared. Scope: Windows-specific commands that alter system configuration, executed from WSL via PowerShell. Consumes ZBUHW tinder constants from `buhw_windows.sh`. Module file: `buwc_windows.sh`, CLI: `buwc_cli.sh`, function prefix: `buwc_*`, colophon family: `buw-wc*`.

**2. Key Line Builder Contract (`zburh_build_key_line`)**
Specify the shared key-line builder in `burh_regime.sh`. This is the wire format contract between all key-install commands:
- Marker format: `# BURH:<alias>` suffix — the idempotency tag
- Output variables: `ZBURH_KEY_LINE` (full authorized_keys line), `ZBURH_KEY_MARKER` (grep target)
- Line format with command= routing: `command="<BURH_COMMAND>" <BURH_SSH_PUBKEY> # BURH:<BURH_ALIAS>`
- Line format without routing: `<BURH_SSH_PUBKEY> # BURH:<BURH_ALIAS>`
- Pure function — no I/O, callers handle file operations per platform

This is the shared contract that both `burh_install_key` (Unix) and `buwc_install_key` (Windows) depend on. Changes to the marker format break idempotency across both platforms.

Prerequisite: complete study phase from ₢A-AAQ (same reference documents apply).

### specify-new-buk-commands-in-bus0 (₢A-AAQ) [rough]

## Character
Spec writing — studying three existing concept model documents, then applying the patterns to produce two new BUS0 subdocuments. Mechanical once the study phase is complete.

## Docket

Write BUS0 subdocuments for the three new BUK commands added in ₢A-AAC. One command per include file.

**Phase 1 — Study (read-only):**
Read these documents for patterns:
- RBS0 (`Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBS0-SpecTop.adoc`) — how RBK structures command specs with include files
- AXLA (`Tools/cmk/vov_veiled/AXLA-Lexicon.adoc`) — motif vocabulary and voicing annotations
- MCM (`Tools/cmk/vov_veiled/MCM-MetaConceptModel.adoc`) — linked term structure: mapping section, anchors, definition sites
- BUSD-DispatchRuntime.adoc — existing BUS0 subdocument precedent

**Phase 2 — Write three subdocuments:**

Architecture context (resolved in ₢A-AAC): `zburh_build_key_line` in `burh_regime.sh` is the shared pure string builder (sets `ZBURH_KEY_LINE` and `ZBURH_KEY_MARKER`). Platform wrappers handle I/O: Unix file ops on Linux/Mac, PowerShell on Windows. Marker format `# BURH:<alias>` is the idempotency wire contract.

Three commands to specify:
1. `burh_install_key` (buw-rhk) — Linux/Mac key install. Uses `${HOME}/.ssh/authorized_keys`, chmod 600.
2. `buwc_install_key` (buw-wck) — Windows admin key install. Uses `ZBUHW_ADMIN_AUTH_KEYS` (PowerShell I/O), icacls for permissions.
3. `buwc_bootstrap_sshd` (buw-wcb) — Windows sshd provisioning. Platform gate (WSL required), `buc_require "OVERWRITE"` confirmation gate. Owns sshd_config, service, firewall. Does NOT touch authorized_keys.

Each subdocument should specify:
- Inputs (regime variables consumed, folio requirements)
- Preconditions (platform gates, confirmation gates)
- Behavior (what it writes, idempotency guarantees)
- Ownership boundary (what it does and does NOT manage)
- Wire formats (marker pattern for key install commands)

Add `include::` lines to BUS0 at appropriate positions.

### practice-environment-procedures (₢A-AAD) [rough]

## Character
Human-driven with agent amending.

## Docket
User executes Environment procedures on Windows host:
- `buw-HWew` — WSL distro creation (`rbtww-main`)
- `buw-HWec` — Cygwin install

Agent amends handbook for issues. Goal: WSL distro and Cygwin both functional, ready for fundus provisioning and Docker.

### practice-fundus-provisioning (₢A-AAE) [rough]

## Character
Human-driven with agent amending.

## Docket
User runs existing JJK fundus provisioning inside the WSL distro:
- `jjw-tfP1.ProvisionPhase1.sh` (sudo, inside WSL) — create `jjfu_*` accounts
- `jjw-tfP2.ProvisionPhase2.sh` — clone repos, install BUK

No new code expected — this validates that the existing `jjfp_fundus.sh` works unchanged inside WSL on Windows. If issues arise (platform detection, path differences, SSH localhost behavior in WSL), agent amends `jjfp_fundus.sh` or documents WSL-specific caveats.

### practice-docker-procedures (₢A-AAF) [rough]

## Character
Human-driven with agent amending.

## Docket
User executes Docker procedures on Windows host:
- `rbw-HWdd` — Docker Desktop install + WSL integration
- `rbw-HWdw` — native dockerd inside WSL distro
- `rbw-HWdc` — docker context discipline (WSL uses native, Windows/Cygwin use Desktop)

Agent amends handbook for issues. Goal: both Docker daemons running, context routing deterministic, `jjfu_*` users can access Docker inside WSL.

### create-verification-tabtargets (₢A-AAH) [rough]

## Character
Design + implementation — informed by practice walkthrough discoveries.

## Docket
Create automation tabtargets that probe Windows host health over SSH. SSH connectivity verification moved to ₢A-AAL (`buw-HWvs`); this pace covers post-SSH infrastructure health.

Candidates (refine based on what practice paces reveal):
- WSL distro health: systemd running, expected packages present
- Fundus accounts: `jjfu_*` users exist and are SSH-reachable
- Docker dual-daemon: both daemons running, context routing correct
- End-to-end: RBK test fixture can charge/quench inside WSL

These are automation tabtargets (exit status matters), not handbooks. They run from the curia and probe the Windows host via SSH — so they depend on ₢A-AAC (practice-access) completing successfully.

Scope will narrow during practice — not all candidates may be worth automating.

### reconsider-cygwin-install-procedure (₢A-AAI) [rough]

## Character
Design review — lightweight judgment call.

## Docket
Revisit whether `buw-HWec` should dictate full Cygwin installation or just configure an existing install. After practicing the other procedures, we'll have better intuition about how much hand-holding the handbook should provide vs assuming prerequisites are already in place.

### adopt-step-autonumber-across-all-procedures (₢A-AAJ) [rough]

## Character
Mechanical sweep with judgment — convert existing procedures, evaluate whether the pattern held up.

## Docket
After practicing the Windows procedures with `buh_step1`/`buh_step2`, decide whether to adopt auto-numbered steps across all handbook procedures (onboarding, payor). Convert if yes, document why not if no. Includes PayorEstablish, PayorRefresh, QuotaBuild, and any onboarding procedures still using hardcoded `buh_section "N. ..."`.

### retest-fundus-after-practice (₢A-AAP) [rough]

## Character
Test execution — regression check after practice-driven amendments.

## Docket
Re-run the full fundus scenario test suite (`tt/jjw-tfs.TestFundusScenario.localhost.sh`) to confirm that any code changes made during practice paces (AAC–AAF) haven't broken the BURH-based bind path.

Same coverage as ₢A-AAO but exercised after practice amendments have landed. If failures surface, fix and iterate.

### handbook-render-param1-coverage (₢A-AAS) [rough]

## Character
Future follow-up to the handbook-render fixture landed in ₣A6 (₢A6AAt). Design decision pace — likely requires some discussion of how to represent per-colophon parameterization in Theurge's test fixtures before any code moves.

## Docket
The handbook-render fixture in ₣A6 exercises 15 of the 16 handbook colophons. The one skipped is `rbw-HWdw` (`RBZ_HW_DOCKER_WSL_NATIVE`), which uses the `param1` channel — it requires a WSL-target argument to run, and the fixture's uniform "invoke and check exit 0" pattern does not supply one.

Decide how the handbook-render fixture should handle this (and any future param1-channel handbooks). Candidate directions:

- **Per-colophon argument lookup**: extend the Rust-side handbook colophon registry with a `default_args` slice per entry. Most entries get `&[]`; `rbw-HWdw` gets a known-safe dummy. Keeps the case loop uniform, localizes the exception.
- **Dedicated subsection in the fixture**: a second section listing only param1 handbooks with their dummy values. Preserves the "one case per colophon" symmetry without threading argument tables through the uniform case.
- **Split fixture**: `handbook-render-simple` (no args) + `handbook-render-parameterized` (args explicit). Heavier and probably not worth it for one offender.

This pace picks a direction, implements it, and extends handbook-render coverage to `rbw-HWdw`.

### Prerequisites
- ₢A6AAt (handbook-render-fixture) must be complete — this pace patches a known scope deferral from that work.

### Wrap criterion
- handbook-render fixture exercises all 16 handbook colophons (including `rbw-HWdw`)
- Pattern generalizes to any future param1 handbooks added via `rbz_zipper.sh`
- Test asserts the chosen dummy param1 value(s) produce a rendering exit (not an argument-missing exit)

### Notes
- Known exit 0 is not the only valid verdict here. The test's purpose is to catch display breakage in handbook rendering — the dummy argument must steer toward a rendered output path, not an early-exit-due-to-bad-arg path.
- `rbw-HWdw` is currently the only param1 handbook. If a second one lands before this pace runs, reassess whether an inline table is still the right shape.

### handbook-render-imprint-coverage (₢A-AAT) [rough]

## Character
Imprint-channel sibling to ₢A-AAS. Design decision pace — the core question is how `buh_tt` and the handbook-render fixture should treat imprint-channel colophons when the handbook is host-agnostic. Likely needs discussion before code lands.

## Docket
`buh_tt` delegates to `buyy_tt_yawp`, which globs `${BURD_TABTARGET_DIR}/${colophon}.*` when no imprint arg is supplied and picks the first alphabetical match. For imprint-channel colophons with multiple host variants, the first match is arbitrary.

### Concrete trigger
Surfaced during ₢A6AAn. In `Tools/rbk/rbh0/rbhw0_top.sh:52`, the Windows handbook teaches Phase 3 with:
```bash
buh_tt "     Phase 2 (clone repos):              " "${JJZ_FUNDUS_PHASE2}"
```
`JJZ_FUNDUS_PHASE2` is enrolled with channel `imprint`; the tabtargets are `jjw-tfP2.ProvisionPhase2.cerebro.sh` and `jjw-tfP2.ProvisionPhase2.localhost.sh`. The handbook is host-agnostic (it's about "a Windows host" in the abstract), so the glob picks `cerebro` — arbitrary and potentially misleading for a learner setting up a different host.

The prior hardcoded string was `tt/jjw-tfP2.ProvisionPhase2.{host}.sh` — explicit about the placeholder. The `buh_tt` replacement (prescribed in ₢A6AAn's docket) loses that teaching signal in exchange for a clickable link.

### Why this isn't caught by the fixture today
`handbook-render` checks exit 0, not rendered content correctness. The Windows handbook rendering with `cerebro.sh` baked in is a green case — the fixture catches display *breakage* but not display *drift*.

### Candidate directions
- **Suffix annotation idiom**: document the standard `buh_tt ... "${FOO}" "" " (pick your host)"` pattern for imprint-channel colophons in host-agnostic handbooks. Cheapest; no primitive change. Accepts that the rendered link points at an arbitrary variant.
- **Multi-variant rendering in `buyy_tt_yawp`**: when called with no imprint on an imprint-channel colophon, render something like `tt/jjw-tfP2.ProvisionPhase2.{host}.sh` (synthetic placeholder) instead of globbing. Needs a way to detect "colophon is imprint-channel" — likely requires `buz_exec_lookup` or a sibling query on the zipper registry.
- **Require explicit imprint**: make `buh_tt` on an imprint-channel colophon without an imprint arg a hard error. Forces every caller to make a choice. Breaking change; audit scope unclear without a grep sweep.
- **Companion fixture assertion**: extend `handbook-render` (coordinating with ₢A-AAS) to snapshot rendered content and diff against expected per-colophon output. Catches drift, not just breakage. Heavier but pairs naturally with the param1 coverage work.

### Prerequisites
- ₢A6AAn complete — it produced the concrete trigger.
- Likely best sequenced after ₢A-AAS so the fixture's parameterization mechanism is known before deciding whether to leverage it for content-snapshot testing.

### Wrap criterion
- Decision recorded on imprint-channel handling in handbooks (one of the directions above, or another).
- If a primitive change: `buh_tt`/`buyy_tt_yawp` updated, call sites audited, fixture updated if behavior is observable.
- If an idiom only: documented in BCG or the handbook-building guide, and `rbhw0_top.sh:52` updated to apply it.
- Windows handbook (`rbw-hw`) renders in a way that doesn't silently commit a learner to the wrong host variant.

### Notes
- Currently `JJZ_FUNDUS_PHASE2` is the only known imprint-channel colophon used from a host-agnostic handbook. `JJZ_FUNDUS_SCENARIO` and `JJZ_FUNDUS_SINGLE` are also imprint-channel but aren't referenced from handbooks at time of slate.
- Pairs thematically with ₢A-AAS — both address the gap where channeled colophons meet uniform handbook/fixture shapes. May be worth groom-time discussion of whether to merge.

File-touch bitmap (x = pace commit touched file):

  1 G upgrade-rbhp-to-buh-combinators
  2 A draft-all-handbook-implementations
  3 B review-handbook-draft
  4 K implement-burh-regime
  5 L wire-handbooks-to-burh
  6 M jjk-spec-burh-bind-in-jjs0
  7 N jjk-implement-burh-bind
  8 O jjk-validate-fundus-scenarios
  9 C practice-access-procedures

Disk survey (threshold 85%): 228 GB: 80%
Heat: rbk-mvp-3-draft-windows-procedures (₣A-)
Status: racing
Created: 260412


## Paces

### practice-access-procedures (₢A-AAC) [rough]

## Character
Human-driven with agent amending — running automation tabtargets on real hardware, discovering gaps.

## Docket
Run the new SSH automation tabtargets against the actual Windows host.

**Mac side:**
1. Run platform constructors per BURH profile — creates `burh.env`, displays `ssh-keygen` commands if pubkey missing
2. User runs `ssh-keygen` manually (copy/paste from constructor output)
3. Re-run constructors — picks up `.pub` files, writes complete `burh.env`
4. `buw-HWsc` — write `~/.ssh/config` entries from all profiles
5. Commit + push (BURH profiles now carry real pubkeys)

**Windows/WSL side:**
Git pull in WSL, then run `buw-HWbs`.

Note: sshd is already configured from manual AccessBase practice (steps 1-7). Running `buw-HWbs` will overwrite `sshd_config` and `authorized_keys` with BURH-derived versions. This validates that automation produces a working result, but replaces manual config. Human decides.

**Verify:**
- `buw-HWvs` per profile — ssh via each alias, confirm correct environment lands
- `jjx_bind` to Windows host — confirm relay works

Agent amends tabtarget code for any issues discovered during practice. This is the first real cross-boundary execution — expect discoveries.

### specify-bus0-buwc-module-and-key-builder (₢A-AAR) [rough]

## Character
Spec writing — adding infrastructure-level definitions to BUS0. Mechanical once patterns are understood from the study phase in ₢A-AAQ.

## Docket

Add two infrastructure-level specifications to BUS0:

**1. BUWC Module Declaration**
Define the Windows Commands module as a category in BUS0, parallel to how BURH Regime is declared. Scope: Windows-specific commands that alter system configuration, executed from WSL via PowerShell. Consumes ZBUHW tinder constants from `buhw_windows.sh`. Module file: `buwc_windows.sh`, CLI: `buwc_cli.sh`, function prefix: `buwc_*`, colophon family: `buw-wc*`.

**2. Key Line Builder Contract (`zburh_build_key_line`)**
Specify the shared key-line builder in `burh_regime.sh`. This is the wire format contract between all key-install commands:
- Marker format: `# BURH:<alias>` suffix — the idempotency tag
- Output variables: `ZBURH_KEY_LINE` (full authorized_keys line), `ZBURH_KEY_MARKER` (grep target)
- Line format with command= routing: `command="<BURH_COMMAND>" <BURH_SSH_PUBKEY> # BURH:<BURH_ALIAS>`
- Line format without routing: `<BURH_SSH_PUBKEY> # BURH:<BURH_ALIAS>`
- Pure function — no I/O, callers handle file operations per platform

This is the shared contract that both `burh_install_key` (Unix) and `buwc_install_key` (Windows) depend on. Changes to the marker format break idempotency across both platforms.

Prerequisite: complete study phase from ₢A-AAQ (same reference documents apply).

### specify-new-buk-commands-in-bus0 (₢A-AAQ) [rough]

## Character
Spec writing — studying three existing concept model documents, then applying the patterns to produce two new BUS0 subdocuments. Mechanical once the study phase is complete.

## Docket

Write BUS0 subdocuments for the three new BUK commands added in ₢A-AAC. One command per include file.

**Phase 1 — Study (read-only):**
Read these documents for patterns:
- RBS0 (`Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBS0-SpecTop.adoc`) — how RBK structures command specs with include files
- AXLA (`Tools/cmk/vov_veiled/AXLA-Lexicon.adoc`) — motif vocabulary and voicing annotations
- MCM (`Tools/cmk/vov_veiled/MCM-MetaConceptModel.adoc`) — linked term structure: mapping section, anchors, definition sites
- BUSD-DispatchRuntime.adoc — existing BUS0 subdocument precedent

**Phase 2 — Write three subdocuments:**

Architecture context (resolved in ₢A-AAC): `zburh_build_key_line` in `burh_regime.sh` is the shared pure string builder (sets `ZBURH_KEY_LINE` and `ZBURH_KEY_MARKER`). Platform wrappers handle I/O: Unix file ops on Linux/Mac, PowerShell on Windows. Marker format `# BURH:<alias>` is the idempotency wire contract.

Three commands to specify:
1. `burh_install_key` (buw-rhk) — Linux/Mac key install. Uses `${HOME}/.ssh/authorized_keys`, chmod 600.
2. `buwc_install_key` (buw-wck) — Windows admin key install. Uses `ZBUHW_ADMIN_AUTH_KEYS` (PowerShell I/O), icacls for permissions.
3. `buwc_bootstrap_sshd` (buw-wcb) — Windows sshd provisioning. Platform gate (WSL required), `buc_require "OVERWRITE"` confirmation gate. Owns sshd_config, service, firewall. Does NOT touch authorized_keys.

Each subdocument should specify:
- Inputs (regime variables consumed, folio requirements)
- Preconditions (platform gates, confirmation gates)
- Behavior (what it writes, idempotency guarantees)
- Ownership boundary (what it does and does NOT manage)
- Wire formats (marker pattern for key install commands)

Add `include::` lines to BUS0 at appropriate positions.

### practice-environment-procedures (₢A-AAD) [rough]

## Character
Human-driven with agent amending.

## Docket
User executes Environment procedures on Windows host:
- `buw-HWew` — WSL distro creation (`rbtww-main`)
- `buw-HWec` — Cygwin install

Agent amends handbook for issues. Goal: WSL distro and Cygwin both functional, ready for fundus provisioning and Docker.

### practice-fundus-provisioning (₢A-AAE) [rough]

## Character
Human-driven with agent amending.

## Docket
User runs existing JJK fundus provisioning inside the WSL distro:
- `jjw-tfP1.ProvisionPhase1.sh` (sudo, inside WSL) — create `jjfu_*` accounts
- `jjw-tfP2.ProvisionPhase2.sh` — clone repos, install BUK

No new code expected — this validates that the existing `jjfp_fundus.sh` works unchanged inside WSL on Windows. If issues arise (platform detection, path differences, SSH localhost behavior in WSL), agent amends `jjfp_fundus.sh` or documents WSL-specific caveats.

### practice-docker-procedures (₢A-AAF) [rough]

## Character
Human-driven with agent amending.

## Docket
User executes Docker procedures on Windows host:
- `rbw-HWdd` — Docker Desktop install + WSL integration
- `rbw-HWdw` — native dockerd inside WSL distro
- `rbw-HWdc` — docker context discipline (WSL uses native, Windows/Cygwin use Desktop)

Agent amends handbook for issues. Goal: both Docker daemons running, context routing deterministic, `jjfu_*` users can access Docker inside WSL.

### create-verification-tabtargets (₢A-AAH) [rough]

## Character
Design + implementation — informed by practice walkthrough discoveries.

## Docket
Create automation tabtargets that probe Windows host health over SSH. SSH connectivity verification moved to ₢A-AAL (`buw-HWvs`); this pace covers post-SSH infrastructure health.

Candidates (refine based on what practice paces reveal):
- WSL distro health: systemd running, expected packages present
- Fundus accounts: `jjfu_*` users exist and are SSH-reachable
- Docker dual-daemon: both daemons running, context routing correct
- End-to-end: RBK test fixture can charge/quench inside WSL

These are automation tabtargets (exit status matters), not handbooks. They run from the curia and probe the Windows host via SSH — so they depend on ₢A-AAC (practice-access) completing successfully.

Scope will narrow during practice — not all candidates may be worth automating.

### reconsider-cygwin-install-procedure (₢A-AAI) [rough]

## Character
Design review — lightweight judgment call.

## Docket
Revisit whether `buw-HWec` should dictate full Cygwin installation or just configure an existing install. After practicing the other procedures, we'll have better intuition about how much hand-holding the handbook should provide vs assuming prerequisites are already in place.

### adopt-step-autonumber-across-all-procedures (₢A-AAJ) [rough]

## Character
Mechanical sweep with judgment — convert existing procedures, evaluate whether the pattern held up.

## Docket
After practicing the Windows procedures with `buh_step1`/`buh_step2`, decide whether to adopt auto-numbered steps across all handbook procedures (onboarding, payor). Convert if yes, document why not if no. Includes PayorEstablish, PayorRefresh, QuotaBuild, and any onboarding procedures still using hardcoded `buh_section "N. ..."`.

### retest-fundus-after-practice (₢A-AAP) [rough]

## Character
Test execution — regression check after practice-driven amendments.

## Docket
Re-run the full fundus scenario test suite (`tt/jjw-tfs.TestFundusScenario.localhost.sh`) to confirm that any code changes made during practice paces (AAC–AAF) haven't broken the BURH-based bind path.

Same coverage as ₢A-AAO but exercised after practice amendments have landed. If failures surface, fix and iterate.

### handbook-render-param1-coverage (₢A-AAS) [rough]

## Character
Future follow-up to the handbook-render fixture landed in ₣A6 (₢A6AAt). Design decision pace — likely requires some discussion of how to represent per-colophon parameterization in Theurge's test fixtures before any code moves.

## Docket
The handbook-render fixture in ₣A6 exercises 15 of the 16 handbook colophons. The one skipped is `rbw-HWdw` (`RBZ_HW_DOCKER_WSL_NATIVE`), which uses the `param1` channel — it requires a WSL-target argument to run, and the fixture's uniform "invoke and check exit 0" pattern does not supply one.

Decide how the handbook-render fixture should handle this (and any future param1-channel handbooks). Candidate directions:

- **Per-colophon argument lookup**: extend the Rust-side handbook colophon registry with a `default_args` slice per entry. Most entries get `&[]`; `rbw-HWdw` gets a known-safe dummy. Keeps the case loop uniform, localizes the exception.
- **Dedicated subsection in the fixture**: a second section listing only param1 handbooks with their dummy values. Preserves the "one case per colophon" symmetry without threading argument tables through the uniform case.
- **Split fixture**: `handbook-render-simple` (no args) + `handbook-render-parameterized` (args explicit). Heavier and probably not worth it for one offender.

This pace picks a direction, implements it, and extends handbook-render coverage to `rbw-HWdw`.

### Prerequisites
- ₢A6AAt (handbook-render-fixture) must be complete — this pace patches a known scope deferral from that work.

### Wrap criterion
- handbook-render fixture exercises all 16 handbook colophons (including `rbw-HWdw`)
- Pattern generalizes to any future param1 handbooks added via `rbz_zipper.sh`
- Test asserts the chosen dummy param1 value(s) produce a rendering exit (not an argument-missing exit)

### Notes
- Known exit 0 is not the only valid verdict here. The test's purpose is to catch display breakage in handbook rendering — the dummy argument must steer toward a rendered output path, not an early-exit-due-to-bad-arg path.
- `rbw-HWdw` is currently the only param1 handbook. If a second one lands before this pace runs, reassess whether an inline table is still the right shape.

### handbook-render-imprint-coverage (₢A-AAT) [rough]

## Character
Imprint-channel sibling to ₢A-AAS. Design decision pace — the core question is how `buh_tt` and the handbook-render fixture should treat imprint-channel colophons when the handbook is host-agnostic. Likely needs discussion before code lands.

## Docket
`buh_tt` delegates to `buyy_tt_yawp`, which globs `${BURD_TABTARGET_DIR}/${colophon}.*` when no imprint arg is supplied and picks the first alphabetical match. For imprint-channel colophons with multiple host variants, the first match is arbitrary.

### Concrete trigger
Surfaced during ₢A6AAn. In `Tools/rbk/rbh0/rbhw0_top.sh:52`, the Windows handbook teaches Phase 3 with:
```bash
buh_tt "     Phase 2 (clone repos):              " "${JJZ_FUNDUS_PHASE2}"
```
`JJZ_FUNDUS_PHASE2` is enrolled with channel `imprint`; the tabtargets are `jjw-tfP2.ProvisionPhase2.cerebro.sh` and `jjw-tfP2.ProvisionPhase2.localhost.sh`. The handbook is host-agnostic (it's about "a Windows host" in the abstract), so the glob picks `cerebro` — arbitrary and potentially misleading for a learner setting up a different host.

The prior hardcoded string was `tt/jjw-tfP2.ProvisionPhase2.{host}.sh` — explicit about the placeholder. The `buh_tt` replacement (prescribed in ₢A6AAn's docket) loses that teaching signal in exchange for a clickable link.

### Why this isn't caught by the fixture today
`handbook-render` checks exit 0, not rendered content correctness. The Windows handbook rendering with `cerebro.sh` baked in is a green case — the fixture catches display *breakage* but not display *drift*.

### Candidate directions
- **Suffix annotation idiom**: document the standard `buh_tt ... "${FOO}" "" " (pick your host)"` pattern for imprint-channel colophons in host-agnostic handbooks. Cheapest; no primitive change. Accepts that the rendered link points at an arbitrary variant.
- **Multi-variant rendering in `buyy_tt_yawp`**: when called with no imprint on an imprint-channel colophon, render something like `tt/jjw-tfP2.ProvisionPhase2.{host}.sh` (synthetic placeholder) instead of globbing. Needs a way to detect "colophon is imprint-channel" — likely requires `buz_exec_lookup` or a sibling query on the zipper registry.
- **Require explicit imprint**: make `buh_tt` on an imprint-channel colophon without an imprint arg a hard error. Forces every caller to make a choice. Breaking change; audit scope unclear without a grep sweep.
- **Companion fixture assertion**: extend `handbook-render` (coordinating with ₢A-AAS) to snapshot rendered content and diff against expected per-colophon output. Catches drift, not just breakage. Heavier but pairs naturally with the param1 coverage work.

### Prerequisites
- ₢A6AAn complete — it produced the concrete trigger.
- Likely best sequenced after ₢A-AAS so the fixture's parameterization mechanism is known before deciding whether to leverage it for content-snapshot testing.

### Wrap criterion
- Decision recorded on imprint-channel handling in handbooks (one of the directions above, or another).
- If a primitive change: `buh_tt`/`buyy_tt_yawp` updated, call sites audited, fixture updated if behavior is observable.
- If an idiom only: documented in BCG or the handbook-building guide, and `rbhw0_top.sh:52` updated to apply it.
- Windows handbook (`rbw-hw`) renders in a way that doesn't silently commit a learner to the wrong host variant.

### Notes
- Currently `JJZ_FUNDUS_PHASE2` is the only known imprint-channel colophon used from a host-agnostic handbook. `JJZ_FUNDUS_SCENARIO` and `JJZ_FUNDUS_SINGLE` are also imprint-channel but aren't referenced from handbooks at time of slate.
- Pairs thematically with ₢A-AAS — both address the gap where channeled colophons meet uniform handbook/fixture shapes. May be worth groom-time discussion of whether to merge.

content.text:
Audit complete. Heat ₣A- mounted, BUS0 §Remote Node Access read, all surfaces surveyed against the backbone. Below is the triage report and proposed restructured pace plan.

---

# Triage Report: ₣A- BURN Backbone Alignment

## A. Tabtarget surfaces (`tt/`)

### Regime ops trio
| Current | Verdict | Target | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `buw-rhl.ListHostProfiles.sh` | **rename** | `buw-rnl.ListNodeProfiles.sh` | BURH→BURN colophon shift. Tabtarget body is a thin launcher — pure rename. |
| `buw-rhr.RenderHostProfile.sh` | **rename** | `buw-rnr.RenderNodeProfile.sh` | Same. |
| `buw-rhv.ValidateHostProfile.sh` | **rename** | `buw-rnv.ValidateNodeProfile.sh` | Same. |

### Constructors (`buw-rhc{c,l,m,p,w,x}`)
| Current | Verdict | Target |
|---|---|---|
| `buw-rhc{c,l,m,p,w,x}.Construct{…}.sh` | **morph** | `buw-npg{c,l,m,p,w,x}.Garrison{…}.sh` |

These do half the Garrison job today — write a privileged `burh.env`, prompt for missing key. Under BUS0, Garrison adds the platform-specific ceremony (sshd provisioning + admin key install for Windows; sshd reachability + key install for Linux/Mac) to the constructor step. The constructor logic is preserved as the profile-write phase inside Garrison.

### Recent splits (just landed)
| Current | Verdict | Disposition |
|---|---|---|
| `buw-wcb.BootstrapSshd.sh` (`buwc_bootstrap_sshd`) | **morph → fold** | Becomes the sshd-provisioning phase inside `buw-npg{c,w,p}` (Windows Garrison). Privileged-only — has no workload counterpart. |
| `buw-wck.InstallKey.sh` (`buwc_install_key`) | **split** | Privileged Windows key install → fold into Windows Garrison. Workload Windows key install → new code under `buw-nwc{c,w,p}` (Windows Conscript), reusing the same `zburn_build_key_line` + `icacls` mechanics. |
| `buw-rhk.InstallKey.sh` (`burh_install_key`) | **split** | Privileged Unix key install → fold into Linux/Mac Garrison. Workload Unix key install → fold into Linux/Mac Conscript. The pure-builder helper `zburh_build_key_line` (renamed `zburn_build_key_line`) **keeps its load-bearing role** — it's the wire-format contract. |

### SSH automation (existing `buw-HW*`)
| Current | Verdict | Disposition |
|---|---|---|
| `buw-HWsc.SshConfig.sh` (`burh_ssh_config`) | **rename + retarget** | Survives as a station-user-managed `~/.ssh/config` aggregator over all BURN profiles (both tiers). Suggested colophon: `buw-rnc.NodeSshConfig.sh` (sits with the regime-ops trio, since its scope is "all profiles", not a single Garrison/Conscript). |
| `buw-HWvs.VerifySsh.sh` (`burh_verify_ssh`) | **split + rename** | Workload-tier verify becomes `buw-nwc <workload-alias>` (BUS0 Check). Privileged verify becomes the post-Garrison verification phase inside Garrison itself, not a separate tabtarget. The existing function's logic survives as Check's implementation. |

### Manual handbook tabtargets (the access trio)
| Current | Verdict |
|---|---|
| `buw-HWab.AccessBase.sh` | **retire** — replaced by `buw-npg{c,w,p}` Windows Garrison automation. |
| `buw-HWar.AccessRemote.sh` | **retire** — covered by constructor (now Garrison-internal) + `buw-rnc` SSH-config aggregator. |
| `buw-HWax.AccessEntrypoints.sh` | **retire** — replaced by `buwc_install_key` mechanic, which Garrison/Conscript invoke. |

### Environment handbook tabtargets (Cygwin / WSL)
| Current | Verdict |
|---|---|
| `buw-HWec.EnvironmentCygwin.sh` | **keep** — install-Cygwin is a prerequisite to Garrison, not part of node-access verbs. Stays in BUK Windows handbook. |
| `buw-HWew.EnvironmentWSL.sh` | **keep** — same reasoning. |

### Handbook landings
| Current | Verdict |
|---|---|
| `buw-h0.HandbookTOP.sh` | **keep + retarget** — content gains pointer to `buw-hn0` node-access landing. |
| `buw-hw.HandbookWindows.sh` | **keep + retarget** — content morphs: replace AccessBase/Remote/Entrypoints index entries with Garrison/Conscript pointers; preserve WSL/Cygwin entries; add console-phase residue tabtarget. |
| **(new)** `buw-hn0.HandbookNode.sh` | **mint** — BUS0 catalog requires this. Top-level handbook landing for node access procedures. |
| **(new)** `buw-HnWk.WindowsConsoleHandshake.sh` (or similar) | **mint** — the residue: operator-at-keyboard ceremony for Windows (enable temp password auth, then bootstrap completes Garrison). Surface lives in BUSNW per spec. |

### RBK side
| Current | Verdict |
|---|---|
| `rbw-HWdd/dw/dc.*.sh` (Docker handbooks) | **keep** unchanged — orthogonal to node-access. |
| `rbw-hw.HandbookWindows.sh` (`rbhw_top` orchestrator) | **morph** — Phase 1 entries (`BUWZ_HW_ACCESS_BASE/REMOTE/ENTRY`) replaced with Garrison pointers. Phase 2 (WSL/Cygwin), Phase 3 (fundus), Phase 4 (Docker) survive. |

## B. Module surfaces (`Tools/buk/`, `Tools/jjk/`)

| Module | Verdict | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `burh_regime.sh` | **rename → `burn_regime.sh`** | All `BURH_*` → `BURN_*`. Add `BURN_TIER` enrollment (`privileged\|workload`). Rename `zburh_build_key_line` → `zburn_build_key_line` and update marker `# BURN:<alias>`. |
| `burh_cli.sh` | **rename → `burn_cli.sh`** + restructure | `burh_validate/render/list` survive (renamed). `burh_construct_*` migrate into Garrison routines (different module, see below). `burh_ssh_config` → `burn_ssh_config` and stays as cross-tier helper. `burh_verify_ssh` → split per above. `burh_install_key` retires as standalone, but `zburn_install_key_unix` helper survives for Garrison/Conscript reuse. |
| `buwc_windows.sh` | **morph → absorbed** | Functions (`buwc_install_key`, `buwc_bootstrap_sshd`) retire as top-level commands and re-emerge as helpers (e.g., `zbusnw_install_admin_key`, `zbusnw_bootstrap_sshd`) called by Windows Garrison. Module file likely renames to `busnw_windows.sh` (BUSN Windows) or is folded into a new `busn_node.sh` umbrella with platform-suffixed helper sets. |
| `buwc_cli.sh` | **retire** | The "BUWC" CLI furnish is subsumed by the new Garrison/Conscript CLIs (likely a single `busn_cli.sh` that dispatches all `buw-n[pw]*` verbs). |
| `buhw_windows.sh` | **split** | The tinder constants (`ZBUHW_SSHD_CONFIG`, `ZBUHW_ADMIN_AUTH_KEYS`, `ZBUHW_CYGWIN_*`, port/firewall names) survive as Windows platform constants, repointed to a new home (`busnw_windows.sh` tinder). The display functions `buhw_access_base/remote/entrypoints` retire. `buhw_environment_wsl/cygwin` survive. `buhw_handbook_top`/`buhw_top` survive with retargeted content. |
| `buhw_cli.sh` | **keep + retarget** | Furnish stays thin; enrollment list updates to drop access trio and add console-handshake residue. May rename if the file's scope changes (BUK Windows handbook). |
| `buwz_zipper.sh` | **rewrite** | Wholesale: drop `BUWZ_RHC_*`, `BUWZ_HW_ACCESS_*`, `BUWZ_WC_*`, `BUWZ_RH_INSTALL_KEY`. Add `BUWZ_RN_*` (regime trio + `buw-rnc` SshConfig), `BUWZ_NPG_*` (Garrison ×6 platforms), `BUWZ_NWC_PLATFORM_*` (Conscript ×6), `BUWZ_NWD/NWI/NWC/NWR/NWS_*` (workload ops), `BUWZ_HN0` (node handbook landing). |
| **(JJK)** `jjrlg_legatio.rs` | **rename in place** | `BURH_*` env-var reads → `BURN_*`. File-path read changes from `<alias>/burh.env` → `<alias>/burn.env`. Identifier rename: `zjjrlg_BurhProfile` → `zjjrlg_BurnProfile`. Plus `jjrm_mcp.rs`, `jjtlg_fundus_scenario.rs`. |
| **(JJK)** `JJS0_JobJockeySpec.adoc`, `JJSTF-test-fundus.adoc` | **rename in place** | All BURH references → BURN. |
| **(CLAUDE context)** `jjk-claude-context.md` | **rename in place** | BURH → BURN throughout. |

## C. Handbook fate summary

**Survives wholesale**: `buw-HWec` (Cygwin install), `buw-HWew` (WSL distro setup) — these are environment prerequisites independent of node-access verbs.

**Survives as residue (single new tabtarget)**: the Windows operator-at-keyboard console phase (enable Remote Login / OpenSSH temp-password auth so Garrison's first SSH can land). BUSNW already declares this lives in the handbook surface, not the spec.

**Morphs (retarget content)**: `buw-h0`, `buw-hw`, `rbw-hw` — same launchers, content updated to point at Garrison/Conscript and the console-handshake residue.

**Deletes wholesale**: `buw-HWab/ar/ax` tabtargets and their `buhw_access_*` display functions — replaced by Garrison automation.

---

# Proposed Pace Plan for ₣A-

The paddock's "post-triage" sequencing (spec → implement → practice walk → downstream practice → regression) holds. But two pending paces (AAR, AAQ) need substantive rework — they were drafted under the BUWC-as-module assumption that BUS0's tier model has now superseded.

## Spec subdoc paces (rework AAR/AAQ; new platform-subdoc paces)

**₢A-AAR** (current: "specify BUWC module + key builder") → **reslate** as:
> **specify-zburn-key-line-builder** — specify the `zburn_build_key_line` pure-string contract in BUS0 backbone (marker format `# BURN:<alias>`, output vars, line shapes with/without command= routing). Drop the BUWC-module half — that vocabulary disappears under the tier model.

**₢A-AAQ** (current: "specify per-command subdocs") → **reslate** as:
> **elaborate-busnw-windows-ceremony** — flesh out BUSNW (currently stub). The §Status section already enumerates the TODO: OpenSSH server provisioning, `administrators_authorized_keys` special case, ACL fixes via `icacls`, temp-password bootstrap handshake. Subsections for Garrison ceremony phases (privileged trust establishment) and Conscript ceremony phases (workload user create + key install via privileged authority). BUSNL/BUSNM stay as stubs until first real target.

Add new pace:
> **₢A-AANEW1 — specify-busn-tier-refusal** — specify in BUS0 backbone that every node-access tabtarget asserts `BURN_TIER` matches its expected tier at entry; specify error format. Currently mentioned in passing ("Tabtargets refuse aliases of the wrong tier at entry") but the assertion contract isn't catalogued.

## Implementation paces (new — slate these in order)

**₢A-AANEW2 — burh-to-burn-rename-buk** *(mechanical sweep)*
- `burh_regime.sh` → `burn_regime.sh`; all `BURH_*` vars → `BURN_*`; `zburh_*` → `zburn_*`.
- Add `BURN_TIER` enrollment with values `privileged|workload`.
- Profile file `burh.env` → `burn.env`; subtree path unchanged (`.buk/users/<station>/<alias>/`).
- `burh_cli.sh` → `burn_cli.sh` (regime ops only — list/render/validate); other functions migrate elsewhere.
- `buwz_zipper.sh` rename `BUWZ_RH_*` enrollments → `BUWZ_RN_*`, colophons `buw-rh*` → `buw-rn*`.
- Rename tabtarget files `tt/buw-rh{l,r,v}.*` → `tt/buw-rn{l,r,v}.*`.
- Existing `buf_ext_alias` fact namespace: confirm rename to `buf_ext_burn` or keep — small load-bearing decision.
- All existing BUK tests must pass after rename. Only mechanical scope.

**₢A-AANEW3 — burh-to-burn-rename-jjk** *(mechanical sweep, parallel to AANEW2)*
- `jjrlg_legatio.rs`: `BURH_HOST/USER/ALIAS` env-var reads → `BURN_*`; `burh.env` path → `burn.env`; identifier `BurhProfile` → `BurnProfile`.
- `jjrm_mcp.rs`, `jjtlg_fundus_scenario.rs`: rename refs.
- `JJS0_JobJockeySpec.adoc`, `JJSTF-test-fundus.adoc`: rename in place.
- `jjk-claude-context.md`: rename.
- `tt/vow-b.Build.sh` + `tt/vow-t.Test.sh` green; fundus scenario test still passes.

**₢A-AANEW4 — garrison-platform-machinery**
- New module `busn_node.sh` (or `burnp_privileged.sh`) with Garrison routines per platform.
- Refactor each constructor (`burh_construct_{linux,mac,cygwin,wsl,powershell,localhost}`) into a Garrison routine that adds the ceremony phase appropriate to the platform.
- Windows Garrison (`busn_garrison_{c,w,p}`) absorbs `buwc_bootstrap_sshd` + `buwc_install_key` as internal phases.
- Linux/Mac Garrison: profile write + sshd reachability check + privileged key install (`zburn_install_key_unix` helper extracted from `burh_install_key`).
- Localhost Garrison: trivial.
- Mint tabtargets `tt/buw-npg{c,l,m,p,w,x}.Garrison{…}.sh`.
- Each Garrison asserts at entry: incoming alias's tier (if existing) is privileged or being newly created.

**₢A-AANEW5 — conscript-discharge-inventory-machinery**
- Conscript routines per platform (`busn_conscript_{c,l,m,p,w,x}`): create remote OS user via privileged authority, install station-user pubkey, write workload `burn.env` profile (with `BURN_TIER=workload`).
- Discharge routine (`busn_discharge`): single tabtarget `buw-nwd <workload-alias>`; uses workload profile's recorded privileged authority to delete remote user.
- Inventory routine (`busn_inventory`): `buw-nwi <privileged-alias>`; lists workload users on the node.
- Workload tier letter chooses workload shell, independent of privileged BURN's shell — the spec is explicit about this decoupling.

**₢A-AANEW6 — workload-operational-tabtargets**
- `buw-nwc <workload-alias>` (Check) — passwordless SSH probe; absorbs the workload portion of `burh_verify_ssh`.
- `buw-nwr <workload-alias> <cmd-file>` (Run) — execute remote, capture to `BURD_OUTPUT_DIR`.
- `buw-nws <workload-alias>` (Ssh) — interactive session.
- Note the colophon proximity: `buw-nwc` (Check) sits right next to `buw-nwc{platform}` (Conscript). Paddock already accepts this.

**₢A-AANEW7 — node-handbook-landing-and-windows-residue**
- Mint `tt/buw-hn0.HandbookNode.sh` and matching display function.
- Mint Windows console-handshake residue tabtarget (operator-at-keyboard temp-password / Remote Login enable, then call back to Garrison).
- Retire `buw-HWab.AccessBase.sh`, `buw-HWar.AccessRemote.sh`, `buw-HWax.AccessEntrypoints.sh` and their `buhw_access_*` display functions.
- Retarget `buw-h0`, `buw-hw`, `rbw-hw` content per audit above.

**₢A-AANEW8 — buw-rnc-ssh-config-aggregator** *(could fold into AANEW2 if scope holds)*
- Rename `buw-HWsc.SshConfig.sh` → `buw-rnc.NodeSshConfig.sh`.
- Function `burh_ssh_config` → `burn_ssh_config` already covered by AANEW2; this pace covers the colophon move and any tier-aware filtering decision (does it emit both privileged + workload entries? Likely yes).

## Practice + regression (existing paces, reslate after implementation)

**₢A-AAC** — reslate the docket with new verbs:
- Mac side: `buw-npg{c,w,p}` to garrison Windows host (3 privileged profiles per shell flavor).
- Windows side: each Garrison runs the console handshake → bootstrap → admin key install → loopback verify in one ceremony. No separate `buw-HWbs` step.
- `buw-rnc` to write `~/.ssh/config` aggregate.
- Optional: `buw-nwc<platform> <privileged-alias> <workload-moniker>` to conscript a workload account; `buw-nwc <workload-alias>` to check.
- Verify with `jjx_bind` (now reading `burn.env`, post-AANEW3 rename).

**₢A-AAD** — practice WSL + Cygwin install — unchanged docket. (`buw-HWec`, `buw-HWew` survive untouched.)

**₢A-AAE** — practice fundus provisioning inside WSL — unchanged docket. (Provisions workload-style accounts; conceptually same as Conscript but JJK-managed for fundus accounts.)

**₢A-AAF** — practice Docker dual-daemon — unchanged docket.

**₢A-AAP** — regression-retest fundus after practice amendments — unchanged docket; runs after AAE.

## Triaged-out (per paddock — keep these as paddock decisions; no slate change needed)
- AAH (verification tabtargets) → folds into practice wraps.
- AAI (reconsider Cygwin install) → folds into AAD wrap notes.
- AAJ (autonumber sweep) → relocate to ₣A6.
- AAS, AAT (handbook-render fixture coverage) → relocate to ₣BB.

---

## Summary of pace deltas

**Reslate**: AAR (drop BUWC half), AAQ (morph to BUSNW elaboration).

**Slate new** (in order):
1. `specify-busn-tier-refusal` (spec)
2. `burh-to-burn-rename-buk` (impl, mechanical)
3. `burh-to-burn-rename-jjk` (impl, mechanical, parallelizable with #2)
4. `garrison-platform-machinery` (impl)
5. `conscript-discharge-inventory-machinery` (impl)
6. `workload-operational-tabtargets` (impl)
7. `node-handbook-landing-and-windows-residue` (impl + retire)
8. `buw-rnc-ssh-config-aggregator` (impl, optional fold into #2)

**Reslate after impl**: AAC (use new verbs), AAD/E/F/P unchanged.

**Relocate** (already in paddock): AAJ → ₣A6; AAS, AAT → ₣BB.

Ready for review. No code or pace state changed.

# jjezs_reslate AAR

## Character
Spec writing — single contract specification. Mechanical.

## Docket
Specify the `zburn_build_key_line` pure-string contract in BUS0 §Remote Node Access. Defines the `# BURN:<alias>` idempotency marker, the output variable shape, and the key-line format with and without `command=` routing. Pure function — no I/O.

This is the wire format for all key-install helpers across Garrison and Conscript. Marker format change breaks idempotency across all platforms.

Original AAR scope minus the BUWC module declaration — that vocabulary disappears under the BUS0 tier model.

# jjezs_reslate AAQ

## Character
Spec writing — fleshing out a stub spec into full ceremony coverage. Mostly mechanical once Garrison/Conscript phase boundaries are clear.

## Docket
Elaborate `Tools/buk/vov_veiled/BUSNW-NodeWindows.adoc` covering Garrison and Conscript ceremonies on Windows.

Garrison phases: console (operator at keyboard, handbook surface only), handshake (first SSH via temp creds), provisioning (sshd_config, service, firewall, admin authorized_keys), harden (disable temp auth, verify keys-only).

Conscript phases per workload shell: remote user create via privileged BURN, per-user authorized_keys (NOT admin auth_keys), workload `burn.env` write, verification.

Wire format details: `administrators_authorized_keys` path + `icacls` ACL contract; per-user authorized_keys path; sshd_config directives Windows OpenSSH refuses (`UsePAM`, `ChallengeResponseAuthentication`).

Codify the PowerShell-from-WSL pattern: bash owns control flow, PowerShell is the Windows syscall layer, one atomic command per call.

Original AAQ scope morphed: per-command subdocs no longer the right shape under the tier model. Per-platform subdoc is the right home.

- - AXLA (`Tools/cmk/vov_veiled/AXLA-Lexicon.adoc`) — motif vocabulary and voicing annotations

- **Phase 2 — Write three subdocuments:**
+ Garrison phases: console (operator at keyboard, handbook surface only), handshake (first SSH via temp creds), provisioning (sshd_config, service, firewall, admin authorized_keys), harden (disable temp auth, verify keys-only).
- Architecture context (resolved in ₢A-AAC): `zburh_build_key_line` in `burh_regime.sh` is the shared pure string builder (sets `ZBURH_KEY_LINE` and `ZBURH_KEY_MARKER`). Platform wrappers handle I/O: Unix file ops on Linux/Mac, PowerShell on Windows. Marker format `# BURH:<alias>` is the idempotency wire contract.
+ Conscript phases per workload shell: remote user create via privileged BURN, per-user authorized_keys (NOT admin auth_keys), workload `burn.env` write, verification.
- Three commands to specify:
- 1. `burh_install_key` (buw-rhk) — Linux/Mac key install. Uses `${HOME}/.ssh/authorized_keys`, chmod 600.
- 2. `buwc_install_key` (buw-wck) — Windows admin key install. Uses `ZBUHW_ADMIN_AUTH_KEYS` (PowerShell I/O), icacls for permissions.
- 3. `buwc_bootstrap_sshd` (buw-wcb) — Windows sshd provisioning. Platform gate (WSL required), `buc_require "OVERWRITE"` confirmation gate. Owns sshd_config, service, firewall. Does NOT touch authorized_keys.
+ Wire format details: `administrators_authorized_keys` path + `icacls` ACL contract; per-user authorized_keys path; sshd_config directives Windows OpenSSH refuses (`UsePAM`, `ChallengeResponseAuthentication`).
- Each subdocument should specify:
- - Inputs (regime variables consumed, folio requirements)
- - Preconditions (platform gates, confirmation gates)
- - Behavior (what it writes, idempotency guarantees)
- - Ownership boundary (what it does and does NOT manage)
- - Wire formats (marker pattern for key install commands)
+ Codify the PowerShell-from-WSL pattern: bash owns control flow, PowerShell is the Windows syscall layer, one atomic command per call.
- Add `include::` lines to BUS0 at appropriate positions.
+ Original AAQ scope morphed: per-command subdocs no longer the right shape under the tier model. Per-platform subdoc is the right home.

- Spec writing — adding infrastructure-level definitions to BUS0. Mechanical once patterns are understood from the study phase in ₢A-AAQ.

- 
- Add two infrastructure-level specifications to BUS0:
- 
- **1. BUWC Module Declaration**
- Define the Windows Commands module as a category in BUS0, parallel to how BURH Regime is declared. Scope: Windows-specific commands that alter system configuration, executed from WSL via PowerShell. Consumes ZBUHW tinder constants from `buhw_windows.sh`. Module file: `buwc_windows.sh`, CLI: `buwc_cli.sh`, function prefix: `buwc_*`, colophon family: `buw-wc*`.
- 
- **2. Key Line Builder Contract (`zburh_build_key_line`)**
- Specify the shared key-line builder in `burh_regime.sh`. This is the wire format contract between all key-install commands:
- - Marker format: `# BURH:<alias>` suffix — the idempotency tag
- - Output variables: `ZBURH_KEY_LINE` (full authorized_keys line), `ZBURH_KEY_MARKER` (grep target)
- - Line format with command= routing: `command="<BURH_COMMAND>" <BURH_SSH_PUBKEY> # BURH:<BURH_ALIAS>`
- - Line format without routing: `<BURH_SSH_PUBKEY> # BURH:<BURH_ALIAS>`
- - Pure function — no I/O, callers handle file operations per platform
+ Specify the `zburn_build_key_line` pure-string contract in BUS0 §Remote Node Access. Defines the `# BURN:<alias>` idempotency marker, the output variable shape, and the key-line format with and without `command=` routing. Pure function — no I/O.
- This is the shared contract that both `burh_install_key` (Unix) and `buwc_install_key` (Windows) depend on. Changes to the marker format break idempotency across both platforms.
+ This is the wire format for all key-install helpers across Garrison and Conscript. Marker format change breaks idempotency across all platforms.
